content.text: Tasks 1-4 are completed from round 1; I'll create round 2 tasks once we start executing. Let me ask the consequential questions before finalizing the plan.

questions.question: How wide should the new rectangular cards be?

options.label: 2-column (Recommended)
options.description: Two cards per row on desktop, one on mobile/tablet. Wide rectangles with breathing room — still scannable.

options.label: 1-column (list-row)
options.description: One full-width card per row. Maximum editorial feel, fewer cards visible per viewport. Reads like a magazine list.

questions.question: What should happen to the FilterChips row (All / Trending / New / Official / Open Source)?

options.label: Drop it entirely (Recommended)
options.description: Trending/New overlap with sort; Official is already visible as a card badge; Free is rarely the differentiator. Five inputs (search, scope, dept, creator, publisher, tags) + sort cover the work.

options.label: Keep, but render as text-link pivots
options.description: Same controls, but match the new quiet filter rail style — no pill fills, underline-on-active. Adds one more row to the rail.

options.label: Fold into the sort menu
options.description: Trending/New/Recent become sort options; Official + Free become checkboxes inside the sort popover. Single dropdown handles all of it.

questions.question: How should the FilterBar look in the new style?

options.label: Text-link rail (Recommended)
options.description: All controls become text-with-chevron style on a single line, separated by middle dots. No borders, no pills, no boxes. Underline marks active.
options.preview: Showing 142     All * Personal * Org * Enterprise * Dept v * Creator v * Publisher v * Tags v        [v Downloads]

options.label: Quiet pills (current style, lighter)
options.description: Keep pill buttons and bordered popover triggers, but reduce contrast, remove labels, single row. Less ambitious change.

input.content:
# Extension list page — elegance pass ## Context Round 1 (already shipped on `feat/ext-list-elegance`, commits 9adb8be..08a1b5c) tightened ExtCard typography, introduced ResultsSummary with dismissible filter chips, relocated SortSelect, softened FilterBar's border, and added ExtGridSkeleton. After review, three issues remain: 1. **The horizontal divider under FilterBar still reads as a hard rule** — even at 60% opacity, the line breaks the editorial flow. 2. **ExtCard still carries too many elements** — the tag pills row and the inline Install button compete with the name and description. The card should read as a preview, not an action panel. 3. **The filter rail at the top is still chunky** — three rows of pill buttons + bordered popover boxes + a redundant quick-filter row (Trending/New/Official/Open Source) make the rail feel utilitarian. Round 2 goes further: cards lose tags + Install (becoming wider-than-tall rectangles naturally as rows fall away), the FilterBar collapses to a single quieter pill row with no border, and the redundant FilterChips row goes away entirely. Sort and dismissible-chip state remain in ResultsSummary as shipped in round 1. ## Round 1 (shipped — reference only) - C1 `9adb8be` — type scale collapsed to 4 sizes; dept moved below desc as text; tag cap +N indicator; size-11 icon; hover shadow - C2 `cff0aea` — ResultsSummary with dismissible chips; Clear all link; count moves out of header - C3 `3fee07a` — SortSelect to ResultsSummary; Scope/Sort labels dropped; FilterBar border softened to 60% - C4 `08a1b5c` — ExtGridSkeleton for first paint ## Approach (round 2) Three more commits on `feat/ext-list-elegance`. Each independently reviewable. ### Commit 5 — `refactor(ext-list): simplify ExtCard to a preview rectangle` **File:** `app/components/extension/ExtCard.vue` Changes: - **Remove the tag pills row entirely** — drop the `
` block, the `visibleTags` / `hiddenTagCount` computeds, and the `tagLabel` + `TAG_VISIBLE_LIMIT` imports. Tag *filtering* is unaffected; cards just stop displaying them. Discovery happens via search + sidebar taxonomy + the active-chip row. - **Remove the InstallButton** from the footer. The whole-card `NuxtLink` already targets the detail page (via the `after:absolute after:inset-0` pseudo-element); install lives on the detail page. Drop the `` markup and the `relative z-10` wrapper. - **Consolidate footer to a single bullet-separated meta line**: `🏢 Dept · ★ rating · ⬇ downloads`. All three on the same row, separated by middle dots, all in `text-[12px]` muted ink (rating + count stay font-mono for digit alignment). Remove the separate dept block above; dept now lives on the meta line. - **Spacing adjustment** — drop the card's internal `gap-3` to `gap-2` and `p-[18px]` to `p-4` so the now-shorter card doesn't feel padded. The card will end up roughly 280×130px on desktop instead of 280×220px — a natural wide rectangle. **File:** `app/components/extension/ExtGridSkeleton.vue` - Drop the tag-pill placeholder row and the install-button placeholder bar - Match new dimensions and spacing so the skeleton silhouette matches the simplified card **File:** `app/components/extension/ExtGrid.vue` - Keep the existing `grid-cols-[repeat(auto-fill,minmax(280px,1fr))] gap-4` — auto-fill at 280px gives 3–4 columns on a standard desktop, which the user has confirmed is the preferred density. The card's new rectangular shape comes from removing rows, not from changing column count. ### Commit 6 — `refactor(ext-list): quiet FilterBar to a single pill row` Visual goal: keep pills + popover triggers (the user opted for "quiet pills" over text-link rails), but lighten contrast, drop the prefix labels, and collapse to a single row. Border-bottom goes away entirely. **File:** `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` - Drop `border-b border-(--color-border)/60 pb-3` entirely; replace with `mb-6` whitespace only — no rule. - Collapse to a single `flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2` row containing: ` `. - TagDrawer's *trigger* now sits inline with the other controls; its *panel* (when open) still expands below the rail as it does today. - Remove the `` reference (deletion comes in commit 7). **File:** `app/components/filters/ScopePills.vue` - Lighten the active state: replace `bg-(--color-ink) text-(--color-bg) border-(--color-ink)` (full inversion) with `bg-(--color-ink)/8 text-(--color-ink) border-(--color-ink)/25 font-semibold` — a tinted, outlined pill rather than a filled one. - Keep inactive style as-is (`border-(--color-border) text-(--color-ink-muted)` with hover bump to ink). - Reduce padding from `px-3 py-1` to `px-2.5 py-0.5` so the row reads as a single line of compact pills. **Files:** `app/components/filters/DeptPicker.vue`, `app/components/filters/CreatorPicker.vue`, `app/components/filters/PublisherPicker.vue` - Drop the `{{ t("filters.dept.label") }}:` prefix from each trigger (was "Department:", "Creator:", "Publisher:"). The popover trigger now shows the category name *when no selection is active* (e.g., `Dept ▾`) or the selected value *when set* (e.g., `Eng / Cloud ▾`). - Reduce trigger padding to `px-2.5 py-0.5` and font to `text-[12px]` to match the new compact pill row. - Soften the active border from `border-(--color-ink)/35` to `border-(--color-ink)/20`. - Selected-state still uses bold text + ink color to mark "this filter is set" — the bold weight does the work the border used to. **File:** `app/components/filters/TagDrawer.vue` - Trigger button: match the new compact pill style (`px-2.5 py-0.5 text-[12px]`). - The expandable panel below stays unchanged (multi-select tag pills, match-mode toggle, clear-tags link all still work as-is). - Keep the accent-tinted count badge when tags are active — it's allowed by decision #11 (filter-active state). ### Commit 7 — `refactor(ext-list): drop FilterChips row` **Files:** - `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` — remove `` reference (already handled in commit 6's reshape; commit 7 finalizes the cleanup if any reference remains). - `app/components/filters/FilterChips.vue` — delete the file. Rationale: - "Trending" / "New" overlap with the sort dropdown (Stars / Downloads / Recent already cover the temporal axis). - "Official" is already visible as a gold badge on every official card — the filter chip is redundant with the visual signal. - "Open Source" / "Free" is rarely the differentiator for a tools marketplace; if it becomes one, add as a toggle in ResultsSummary later. The `filter` URL param still validates against the schema — old bookmarked URLs (`?filter=trending`) keep working and surface as a dismissible chip in ResultsSummary. We just stop *setting* it from the UI. i18n keys (`filters.chips.*`) are left in place — they cost nothing and the dismissible chip still uses them. ## Critical files **Edited:** - `app/components/extension/ExtCard.vue` — commit 5 - `app/components/extension/ExtGridSkeleton.vue` — commit 5 - `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` — commit 6 (and final cleanup in 7) - `app/components/filters/ScopePills.vue` — commit 6 - `app/components/filters/DeptPicker.vue` — commit 6 - `app/components/filters/CreatorPicker.vue` — commit 6 - `app/components/filters/PublisherPicker.vue` — commit 6 - `app/components/filters/TagDrawer.vue` — commit 6 **Deleted:** - `app/components/filters/FilterChips.vue` — commit 7 **Untouched (verified still needed):** - `app/pages/extensions/index.vue` — header + ResultsSummary + ExtGrid wiring stays as round 1 shipped - `app/components/extension/ResultsSummary.vue` — the dismissible `filter` chip stays in place for old-URL forward compat ## Reused utilities (round 2) - `formatCount()` and `deptPath()` / `MY_DEPT_ID` — all still used by ExtCard's simplified meta line - `useFilters()` — unchanged - CSS vars (`--color-ink`, `--color-ink-muted`, `--color-border`, `--color-accent`, `--color-card`, `--radius-card`) — unchanged ## Accent budget Post-round-2, the list page's accent moments are: - ✅ Card hover border tint (unchanged) - ✅ TagDrawer active filter count badge (unchanged) - ✅ "View Panorama" link (unchanged) - ✅ Dept "Mine" emphasis on cards (text-only accent, kept from round 1) - ❌ ResultsSummary chips: neutral - ❌ Scope pills: now ink-tinted active state, no accent **The InstallButton is gone from the list page** — install only exists on detail pages now. This removes the strongest accent footprint from the list. Net: significantly less accent ink on the page, which fits decision #11's "one intentional accent moment per viewport" goal. ## Verification 1. **Local dev**: `bun dev` → open `http://localhost:3000/en/extensions`. 2. **Card structure**: confirm cards show icon + name + badge on the header row, 2-line description, and a single meta line (`🏢 Dept · ★ N.N · ⬇ count`). No tag pills, no Install button on the card. 3. **Whole-card click**: clicking anywhere on a card body navigates to the detail page where InstallButton still exists. 4. **Filter bar**: confirm the rail is a single line of compact pills (Scope pills + Dept/Creator/Publisher/Tags triggers). No horizontal rule below. 5. **Tag drawer**: clicking "Tags ▾" still expands the multi-select panel; selecting tags still adds them as filters and dismissible chips appear in ResultsSummary. 6. **FilterChips gone**: no Trending/New/Official/Free pill row anywhere. URL `/en/extensions?filter=trending` still loads cleanly; the dismissible chip in ResultsSummary still appears (forward compat). 7. **Picker trigger labels**: when no selection, triggers show category name only (`Dept ▾`); when a selection is active, they show the selected value (`Eng / Cloud ▾`) in bold. 8. **Locale parity**: switch to `/zh/extensions` — card meta line and pickers render in Chinese. 9. **Theme parity**: Ivory → Dark → Mono Clean — all three should render the new lighter pill active state without darkness/contrast issues. Dark theme's lighter scope pill (`bg-ink/8`) may need an opacity bump (`/15` or `/20`) — adjust if needed during implementation. 10. **Skeleton**: hard-reload `/en/extensions` — skeleton silhouette should match the new card (no tag bars, no install bar, single meta line). 11. **Validate**: `bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun run test`. ## Out of scope - Pagination polish, infinite scroll - Density toggle (list vs. grid view) - Card SaveButton / favorites surfacing on hover - Filter persistence across sessions - Sidebar refinement - Detail page changes

# Extension list page — elegance pass ## Context Round 1 (already shipped on `feat/ext-list-elegance`, commits 9adb8be..08a1b5c) tightened ExtCard typography, introduced ResultsSummary with dismissible filter chips, relocated SortSelect, softened FilterBar's border, and added ExtGridSkeleton. After review, three issues remain: 1. **The horizontal divider under FilterBar still reads as a hard rule** — even at 60% opacity, the line breaks the editorial flow. 2. **ExtCard still carries too many elements** — the tag pills row and the inline Install button compete with the name and description. The card should read as a preview, not an action panel. 3. **The filter rail at the top is still chunky** — three rows of pill buttons + bordered popover boxes + a redundant quick-filter row (Trending/New/Official/Open Source) make the rail feel utilitarian. Round 2 goes further: cards lose tags + Install (becoming wider-than-tall rectangles naturally as rows fall away), the FilterBar collapses to a single quieter pill row with no border, and the redundant FilterChips row goes away entirely. Sort and dismissible-chip state remain in ResultsSummary as shipped in round 1. ## Round 1 (shipped — reference only) - C1 `9adb8be` — type scale collapsed to 4 sizes; dept moved below desc as text; tag cap +N indicator; size-11 icon; hover shadow - C2 `cff0aea` — ResultsSummary with dismissible chips; Clear all link; count moves out of header - C3 `3fee07a` — SortSelect to ResultsSummary; Scope/Sort labels dropped; FilterBar border softened to 60% - C4 `08a1b5c` — ExtGridSkeleton for first paint ## Approach (round 2) Three more commits on `feat/ext-list-elegance`. Each independently reviewable. ### Commit 5 — `refactor(ext-list): simplify ExtCard to a preview rectangle` **File:** `app/components/extension/ExtCard.vue` Changes: - **Remove the tag pills row entirely** — drop the `
` block, the `visibleTags` / `hiddenTagCount` computeds, and the `tagLabel` + `TAG_VISIBLE_LIMIT` imports. Tag *filtering* is unaffected; cards just stop displaying them. Discovery happens via search + sidebar taxonomy + the active-chip row. - **Remove the InstallButton** from the footer. The whole-card `NuxtLink` already targets the detail page (via the `after:absolute after:inset-0` pseudo-element); install lives on the detail page. Drop the `` markup and the `relative z-10` wrapper. - **Consolidate footer to a single bullet-separated meta line**: `🏢 Dept · ★ rating · ⬇ downloads`. All three on the same row, separated by middle dots, all in `text-[12px]` muted ink (rating + count stay font-mono for digit alignment). Remove the separate dept block above; dept now lives on the meta line. - **Spacing adjustment** — drop the card's internal `gap-3` to `gap-2` and `p-[18px]` to `p-4` so the now-shorter card doesn't feel padded. The card will end up roughly 280×130px on desktop instead of 280×220px — a natural wide rectangle. **File:** `app/components/extension/ExtGridSkeleton.vue` - Drop the tag-pill placeholder row and the install-button placeholder bar - Match new dimensions and spacing so the skeleton silhouette matches the simplified card **File:** `app/components/extension/ExtGrid.vue` - Keep the existing `grid-cols-[repeat(auto-fill,minmax(280px,1fr))] gap-4` — auto-fill at 280px gives 3–4 columns on a standard desktop, which the user has confirmed is the preferred density. The card's new rectangular shape comes from removing rows, not from changing column count. ### Commit 6 — `refactor(ext-list): quiet FilterBar to a single pill row` Visual goal: keep pills + popover triggers (the user opted for "quiet pills" over text-link rails), but lighten contrast, drop the prefix labels, and collapse to a single row. Border-bottom goes away entirely. **File:** `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` - Drop `border-b border-(--color-border)/60 pb-3` entirely; replace with `mb-6` whitespace only — no rule. - Collapse to a single `flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2` row containing: ` `. - TagDrawer's *trigger* now sits inline with the other controls; its *panel* (when open) still expands below the rail as it does today. - Remove the `` reference (deletion comes in commit 7). **File:** `app/components/filters/ScopePills.vue` - Lighten the active state: replace `bg-(--color-ink) text-(--color-bg) border-(--color-ink)` (full inversion) with `bg-(--color-ink)/8 text-(--color-ink) border-(--color-ink)/25 font-semibold` — a tinted, outlined pill rather than a filled one. - Keep inactive style as-is (`border-(--color-border) text-(--color-ink-muted)` with hover bump to ink). - Reduce padding from `px-3 py-1` to `px-2.5 py-0.5` so the row reads as a single line of compact pills. **Files:** `app/components/filters/DeptPicker.vue`, `app/components/filters/CreatorPicker.vue`, `app/components/filters/PublisherPicker.vue` - Drop the `{{ t("filters.dept.label") }}:` prefix from each trigger (was "Department:", "Creator:", "Publisher:"). The popover trigger now shows the category name *when no selection is active* (e.g., `Dept ▾`) or the selected value *when set* (e.g., `Eng / Cloud ▾`). - Reduce trigger padding to `px-2.5 py-0.5` and font to `text-[12px]` to match the new compact pill row. - Soften the active border from `border-(--color-ink)/35` to `border-(--color-ink)/20`. - Selected-state still uses bold text + ink color to mark "this filter is set" — the bold weight does the work the border used to. **File:** `app/components/filters/TagDrawer.vue` - Trigger button: match the new compact pill style (`px-2.5 py-0.5 text-[12px]`). - The expandable panel below stays unchanged (multi-select tag pills, match-mode toggle, clear-tags link all still work as-is). - Keep the accent-tinted count badge when tags are active — it's allowed by decision #11 (filter-active state). ### Commit 7 — `refactor(ext-list): drop FilterChips row` **Files:** - `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` — remove `` reference (already handled in commit 6's reshape; commit 7 finalizes the cleanup if any reference remains). - `app/components/filters/FilterChips.vue` — delete the file. Rationale: - "Trending" / "New" overlap with the sort dropdown (Stars / Downloads / Recent already cover the temporal axis). - "Official" is already visible as a gold badge on every official card — the filter chip is redundant with the visual signal. - "Open Source" / "Free" is rarely the differentiator for a tools marketplace; if it becomes one, add as a toggle in ResultsSummary later. The `filter` URL param still validates against the schema — old bookmarked URLs (`?filter=trending`) keep working and surface as a dismissible chip in ResultsSummary. We just stop *setting* it from the UI. i18n keys (`filters.chips.*`) are left in place — they cost nothing and the dismissible chip still uses them. ## Critical files **Edited:** - `app/components/extension/ExtCard.vue` — commit 5 - `app/components/extension/ExtGridSkeleton.vue` — commit 5 - `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` — commit 6 (and final cleanup in 7) - `app/components/filters/ScopePills.vue` — commit 6 - `app/components/filters/DeptPicker.vue` — commit 6 - `app/components/filters/CreatorPicker.vue` — commit 6 - `app/components/filters/PublisherPicker.vue` — commit 6 - `app/components/filters/TagDrawer.vue` — commit 6 **Deleted:** - `app/components/filters/FilterChips.vue` — commit 7 **Untouched (verified still needed):** - `app/pages/extensions/index.vue` — header + ResultsSummary + ExtGrid wiring stays as round 1 shipped - `app/components/extension/ResultsSummary.vue` — the dismissible `filter` chip stays in place for old-URL forward compat ## Reused utilities (round 2) - `formatCount()` and `deptPath()` / `MY_DEPT_ID` — all still used by ExtCard's simplified meta line - `useFilters()` — unchanged - CSS vars (`--color-ink`, `--color-ink-muted`, `--color-border`, `--color-accent`, `--color-card`, `--radius-card`) — unchanged ## Accent budget Post-round-2, the list page's accent moments are: - ✅ Card hover border tint (unchanged) - ✅ TagDrawer active filter count badge (unchanged) - ✅ "View Panorama" link (unchanged) - ✅ Dept "Mine" emphasis on cards (text-only accent, kept from round 1) - ❌ ResultsSummary chips: neutral - ❌ Scope pills: now ink-tinted active state, no accent **The InstallButton is gone from the list page** — install only exists on detail pages now. This removes the strongest accent footprint from the list. Net: significantly less accent ink on the page, which fits decision #11's "one intentional accent moment per viewport" goal. ## Verification 1. **Local dev**: `bun dev` → open `http://localhost:3000/en/extensions`. 2. **Card structure**: confirm cards show icon + name + badge on the header row, 2-line description, and a single meta line (`🏢 Dept · ★ N.N · ⬇ count`). No tag pills, no Install button on the card. 3. **Whole-card click**: clicking anywhere on a card body navigates to the detail page where InstallButton still exists. 4. **Filter bar**: confirm the rail is a single line of compact pills (Scope pills + Dept/Creator/Publisher/Tags triggers). No horizontal rule below. 5. **Tag drawer**: clicking "Tags ▾" still expands the multi-select panel; selecting tags still adds them as filters and dismissible chips appear in ResultsSummary. 6. **FilterChips gone**: no Trending/New/Official/Free pill row anywhere. URL `/en/extensions?filter=trending` still loads cleanly; the dismissible chip in ResultsSummary still appears (forward compat). 7. **Picker trigger labels**: when no selection, triggers show category name only (`Dept ▾`); when a selection is active, they show the selected value (`Eng / Cloud ▾`) in bold. 8. **Locale parity**: switch to `/zh/extensions` — card meta line and pickers render in Chinese. 9. **Theme parity**: Ivory → Dark → Mono Clean — all three should render the new lighter pill active state without darkness/contrast issues. Dark theme's lighter scope pill (`bg-ink/8`) may need an opacity bump (`/15` or `/20`) — adjust if needed during implementation. 10. **Skeleton**: hard-reload `/en/extensions` — skeleton silhouette should match the new card (no tag bars, no install bar, single meta line). 11. **Validate**: `bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun run test`. ## Out of scope - Pagination polish, infinite scroll - Density toggle (list vs. grid view) - Card SaveButton / favorites surfacing on hover - Filter persistence across sessions - Sidebar refinement - Detail page changes

lines: -The list page (`/en/extensions`) is functionally complete but reads as utilitarian rather than editorial. Four issues surface on inspection:

+Round 1 (already shipped on `feat/ext-list-elegance`, commits 9adb8be..08a1b5c) tightened ExtCard typography, introduced ResultsSummary with dismissible filter chips, relocated SortSelect, softened FilterBar's border, and added ExtGridSkeleton. After review, three issues remain:

-1. **ExtCard has fragmented typography** — seven micro font-sizes (10/10.5/11/12/12.5/13/14px) and a two-row header (name+badge on row 1, dept-pill on row 2) make cards feel busy. Tag pills aren't capped — cards with 6+ tags overwhelm the description.

-2. **No active-filter summary** — once filters are applied, the only feedback is the raw count. There's no editorial line communicating *what* is filtered, and no per-chip dismiss affordance. "Clear filters" only appears in the empty state.

-3. **FilterBar chrome is heavy** — three rows, two utilitarian labels ("Scope:", "Sort:"), and a hard border-bottom. Sort logically describes *output* (how results are ordered) but currently sits inside the *input* bar.
-4. **No skeleton on first paint** — the initial render shows nothing until `useFetch` resolves; subsequent navigations keep previous cards visible.
+1. **The horizontal divider under FilterBar still reads as a hard rule** — even at 60% opacity, the line breaks the editorial flow.
+2. **ExtCard still carries too many elements** — the tag pills row and the inline Install button compete with the name and description. The card should read as a preview, not an action panel.

+3. **The filter rail at the top is still chunky** — three rows of pill buttons + bordered popover boxes + a redundant quick-filter row (Trending/New/Official/Open Source) make the rail feel utilitarian.
-Goal: tighten the page into something that reads as deliberately composed — without changing what's possible. Same filters, same data, more elegant surface. One PR, several commits.

+Round 2 goes further: cards lose tags + Install (becoming wider-than-tall rectangles naturally as rows fall away), the FilterBar collapses to a single quieter pill row with no border, and the redundant FilterChips row goes away entirely. Sort and dismissible-chip state remain in ResultsSummary as shipped in round 1.

-Four commits, each independently reviewable. Sort is relocated from FilterBar (input) to a new ResultsSummary band (output), where it sits next to the count and dismissible active-filter chips. ExtCard's type scale collapses from seven sizes to four. Skeleton lands last.
+- C1 `9adb8be` — type scale collapsed to 4 sizes; dept moved below desc as text; tag cap +N indicator; size-11 icon; hover shadow
+- C2 `cff0aea` — ResultsSummary with dismissible chips; Clear all link; count moves out of header
+- C3 `3fee07a` — SortSelect to ResultsSummary; Scope/Sort labels dropped; FilterBar border softened to 60%
-### Commit 1 — `refactor(ext-list): tighten ExtCard hierarchy and typography`
+Three more commits on `feat/ext-list-elegance`. Each independently reviewable.
+### Commit 5 — `refactor(ext-list): simplify ExtCard to a preview rectangle`
-- **Header row**: collapse name+badge+dept onto a single line. Icon box + (name · badge) inline. Move dept out of the icon-aligned column.

-- **Dept placement**: render dept as plain text (Building2 icon + leaf label) on a dedicated line *below* the description, in `text-(--color-ink-muted) text-[11px]`. Drops the bordered pill. "Mine" emphasis becomes `text-(--color-accent)` instead of border+bg tint (still allowed by decision #11 since accent on text only is consistent with how accent links read).
-  - *Alternative kept in reserve*: if dept-as-text feels too quiet, restore a faint left-rule (`border-l border-(--color-ink)/15 pl-2`) instead of the pill.
-- **Tag cap**: limit visible tag pills to 3, append `+N` indicator (same styling, but `border-(--color-border)/60 text-(--color-ink-muted)/70`). Avoids 6-tag cards eating two lines.

-- **Hover refinement**: add `hover:shadow-[0_2px_8px_-4px_oklch(0_0_0_/_0.08)]` for a subtle crafted lift on top of the existing `-translate-y-0.5 + border-accent/30`. Skip in dark theme via CSS layer or accept the muted result (shadow on dark cards is barely visible anyway).
-- **Icon box**: bump to size-11 (was size-10) to balance the larger name. Keep the tinted background+border formula.
-- **Badge weight**: reduce from `font-bold` to `font-semibold` and lower contrast slightly so the name stays primary.

+- **Remove the tag pills row entirely** — drop the `<div v-if="ext.tagIds.length > 0">` block, the `visibleTags` / `hiddenTagCount` computeds, and the `tagLabel` + `TAG_VISIBLE_LIMIT` imports. Tag *filtering* is unaffected; cards just stop displaying them. Discovery happens via search + sidebar taxonomy + the active-chip row.

+- **Remove the InstallButton** from the footer. The whole-card `NuxtLink` already targets the detail page (via the `after:absolute after:inset-0` pseudo-element); install lives on the detail page. Drop the `<InstallButton>` markup and the `relative z-10` wrapper.

+- **Consolidate footer to a single bullet-separated meta line**: `🏢 Dept · ★ rating · ⬇ downloads`. All three on the same row, separated by middle dots, all in `text-[12px]` muted ink (rating + count stay font-mono for digit alignment). Remove the separate dept block above; dept now lives on the meta line.

+- **Spacing adjustment** — drop the card's internal `gap-3` to `gap-2` and `p-[18px]` to `p-4` so the now-shorter card doesn't feel padded. The card will end up roughly 280×130px on desktop instead of 280×220px — a natural wide rectangle.
-No prop changes; component contract is identical.
-### Commit 2 — `feat(ext-list): add ResultsSummary with active filter chips`
+- Drop the tag-pill placeholder row and the install-button placeholder bar
+- Match new dimensions and spacing so the skeleton silhouette matches the simplified card
-Renders a horizontal band that sits between FilterBar and ExtGrid. Layout:

+- Keep the existing `grid-cols-[repeat(auto-fill,minmax(280px,1fr))] gap-4` — auto-fill at 280px gives 3–4 columns on a standard desktop, which the user has confirmed is the preferred density. The card's new rectangular shape comes from removing rows, not from changing column count.
+### Commit 6 — `refactor(ext-list): quiet FilterBar to a single pill row`

+Visual goal: keep pills + popover triggers (the user opted for "quiet pills" over text-link rails), but lighten contrast, drop the prefix labels, and collapse to a single row. Border-bottom goes away entirely.
-Internally uses `useFilters()` (`filters` + `update`) and `useI18n()`. Resolves display names by joining facet arrays:
+- Drop `border-b border-(--color-border)/60 pb-3` entirely; replace with `mb-6` whitespace only — no rule.
+- Collapse to a single `flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2` row containing: `<ScopePills /> <DeptPicker /> <CreatorPicker /> <PublisherPicker /> <TagDrawer />`.
+- TagDrawer's *trigger* now sits inline with the other controls; its *panel* (when open) still expands below the rail as it does today.
+- Remove the `<FilterChips />` reference (deletion comes in commit 7).
-- `query` → just shows the quoted string, dismiss = `update({ q: undefined })`
-- `tags` → one chip per tag id, label via `tagLabel(id, locale)` from `shared/tags.ts`
-- `tagMatch` → not directly removable (derived from tags presence; clears when last tag clears)
-- `category`, `funcCat`, `subCat`, `l2` → out of scope for this band (they're set from sidebar nav, not filters UI); skip
-Chip styling: small pill with × icon (lucide `X`), neutral border, hover darkens border + ink. No accent fill (per decision #11 — these are state indicators, not active CTAs).

+- Lighten the active state: replace `bg-(--color-ink) text-(--color-bg) border-(--color-ink)` (full inversion) with `bg-(--color-ink)/8 text-(--color-ink) border-(--color-ink)/25 font-semibold` — a tinted, outlined pill rather than a filled one.
+- Keep inactive style as-is (`border-(--color-border) text-(--color-ink-muted)` with hover bump to ink).
+- Reduce padding from `px-3 py-1` to `px-2.5 py-0.5` so the row reads as a single line of compact pills.

-"Clear all" link: shown only when `activeChipsCount > 0`. Calls `update({ q: undefined, scope: undefined, dept: undefined, creator: undefined, publisher: undefined, filter: undefined, tags: undefined, tagMatch: undefined })`.
-When zero filters active, the band shows only the count on the left and Sort on the right — same height, so the page doesn't jump as filters change.

+- Drop the `{{ t("filters.dept.label") }}:` prefix from each trigger (was "Department:", "Creator:", "Publisher:"). The popover trigger now shows the category name *when no selection is active* (e.g., `Dept ▾`) or the selected value *when set* (e.g., `Eng / Cloud ▾`).
+- Reduce trigger padding to `px-2.5 py-0.5` and font to `text-[12px]` to match the new compact pill row.
+- Soften the active border from `border-(--color-ink)/35` to `border-(--color-ink)/20`.
+- Selected-state still uses bold text + ink color to mark "this filter is set" — the bold weight does the work the border used to.

-- Remove the `

` count line from the header — `ResultsSummary` owns it now
-- Remove the `filtersActive` computed (ResultsSummary derives this internally)
-- Header becomes: title alone on the left, Panorama link on the right (when MCP filter active)
+- Trigger button: match the new compact pill style (`px-2.5 py-0.5 text-[12px]`).
+- The expandable panel below stays unchanged (multi-select tag pills, match-mode toggle, clear-tags link all still work as-is).
+- Keep the accent-tinted count badge when tags are active — it's allowed by decision #11 (filter-active state).
-(`zh` mirrors: `"showing": "显示 {count} 个"`, `"clearAll": "清除全部"`, etc.)
-### Commit 3 — `refactor(ext-list): move SortSelect to ResultsSummary, soften FilterBar`

-- `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` — remove third row's `<SortSelect />`; keep only `<FilterChips />` on row 3 (now a single-control row, becomes `justify-start`). Soften the bottom border: `border-b border-(--color-border)/60 pb-3` (was `pb-4`).
-- `app/components/filters/ScopePills.vue` — remove the `Scope:` label; rely on the four button names being self-evident.
-- `app/components/filters/SortSelect.vue` — drop the `Sort:` label, replace with a lucide `ArrowDownUp` icon prefix; keep the `
-No behavioral change — Sort still writes to `filters.sort`.
+- `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` — remove `<FilterChips />` reference (already handled in commit 6's reshape; commit 7 finalizes the cleanup if any reference remains).
-### Commit 4 — `feat(ext-list): add ExtGridSkeleton for first paint`
+- "Trending" / "New" overlap with the sort dropdown (Stars / Downloads / Recent already cover the temporal axis).
+- "Official" is already visible as a gold badge on every official card — the filter chip is redundant with the visual signal.
+- "Open Source" / "Free" is rarely the differentiator for a tools marketplace; if it becomes one, add as a toggle in ResultsSummary later.
-Renders 8 placeholder cards using the same `grid-cols-[repeat(auto-fill,minmax(280px,1fr))] gap-4` shell. Each placeholder mimics ExtCard's silhouette:

+The `filter` URL param still validates against the schema — old bookmarked URLs (`?filter=trending`) keep working and surface as a dismissible chip in ResultsSummary. We just stop *setting* it from the UI.
-- Two stacked lines for name + description (rounded bars, `bg-(--color-border)/40`)
-- Footer line with two short bars + button-sized bar on the right
+i18n keys (`filters.chips.*`) are left in place — they cost nothing and the dismissible chip still uses them.
-Uses Tailwind's `animate-pulse` for the subtle breathing effect.
-- Destructure `pending` from `useFetch`: `const { data, pending, refresh } = await useFetch(...)`

-This only shows on first paint where SSR is not in play (e.g., client-side navigation from an SPA route, or hard refresh with hydration mismatch). With SSR enabled it's effectively a defense-in-depth — useful when fetch fails or is slow but data not yet hydrated.
+- `app/pages/extensions/index.vue` — header + ResultsSummary + ExtGrid wiring stays as round 1 shipped
+- `app/components/extension/ResultsSummary.vue` — the dismissible `filter` chip stays in place for old-URL forward compat
-- `useFilters()` (`app/composables/useFilters.ts:10`) — `filters` reactive ref + `update(partial)` mutator. ResultsSummary chip dismissal calls `update({ key: undefined })`.
-- `deptPath(id, locale)` and `findDept(id)` (`shared/data/departments.ts`) — for dept chip label.
-- Existing CSS vars (`--color-ink`, `--color-ink-muted`, `--color-border`, `--color-accent`, `--color-card`, `--radius-card`) — all three themes already define them.
+- `formatCount()` and `deptPath()` / `MY_DEPT_ID` — all still used by ExtCard's simplified meta line
-The list page already lives within the budget. After this pass:
-- ✅ Dept "Mine" emphasis: now text-only accent (was border+bg) — strictly lighter accent footprint
-- ❌ ResultsSummary chips: deliberately neutral, not tinted
+Post-round-2, the list page's accent moments are:
-Net result: equal or slightly *less* accent tint than today.
+- ✅ Dept "Mine" emphasis on cards (text-only accent, kept from round 1)
+- ❌ Scope pills: now ink-tinted active state, no accent

+**The InstallButton is gone from the list page** — install only exists on detail pages now. This removes the strongest accent footprint from the list. Net: significantly less accent ink on the page, which fits decision #11's "one intentional accent moment per viewport" goal.

-2. **Empty state**: search for `xyzxyz` — verify ExtGrid empty card still appears; ResultsSummary should hide "Clear all" iff `filtersActive` is false (the search itself is a filter, so should show with a `×"xyzxyz"` chip).

-3. **All filter chip types**: apply scope=org, dept=eng.cloud, creator=<first creator>, publisher=<first org>, filter=trending, tags=[a,b], q="claude" — verify each renders as a dismissible chip with the correct localized label, and dismissing each one removes only that filter.
-4. **Clear all**: with multiple filters set, click "Clear all" — URL should drop to `/en/extensions` (no query string).
-5. **Sort relocation**: change Sort to Stars from the ResultsSummary band — verify URL gains `?sort=stars` and the grid re-orders.
-6. **Locale parity**: switch to `/zh/extensions` — chips and "Clear all" render in Chinese; dept name uses `nameZh`.
-7. **Theme parity**: cycle Ivory → Dark → Mono Clean. Card hover shadow may not appear in Dark (expected); skeleton placeholders should be visible in all three.
-8. **Tag cap**: find an extension with ≥4 tags (or seed one) — confirm only 3 pills render with `+N` indicator.
-9. **Mobile**: collapse viewport to 375px — ResultsSummary chips should wrap; FilterBar pickers should already wrap (existing behavior preserved).
-10. **Type-check + lint**: `bun run typecheck && bun run lint` per `docs/plan.md` validate pipeline.

-11. **Coverage**: no logic changes — existing tests should pass without modification. ResultsSummary is presentational; chip-dismiss behavior is exercised through `useFilters` which is already covered.
+2. **Card structure**: confirm cards show icon + name + badge on the header row, 2-line description, and a single meta line (`🏢 Dept · ★ N.N · ⬇ count`). No tag pills, no Install button on the card.
+3. **Whole-card click**: clicking anywhere on a card body navigates to the detail page where InstallButton still exists.
+4. **Filter bar**: confirm the rail is a single line of compact pills (Scope pills + Dept/Creator/Publisher/Tags triggers). No horizontal rule below.
+5. **Tag drawer**: clicking "Tags ▾" still expands the multi-select panel; selecting tags still adds them as filters and dismissible chips appear in ResultsSummary.

+6. **FilterChips gone**: no Trending/New/Official/Free pill row anywhere. URL `/en/extensions?filter=trending` still loads cleanly; the dismissible chip in ResultsSummary still appears (forward compat).
+7. **Picker trigger labels**: when no selection, triggers show category name only (`Dept ▾`); when a selection is active, they show the selected value (`Eng / Cloud ▾`) in bold.
+8. **Locale parity**: switch to `/zh/extensions` — card meta line and pickers render in Chinese.

+9. **Theme parity**: Ivory → Dark → Mono Clean — all three should render the new lighter pill active state without darkness/contrast issues. Dark theme's lighter scope pill (`bg-ink/8`) may need an opacity bump (`/15` or `/20`) — adjust if needed during implementation.
+10. **Skeleton**: hard-reload `/en/extensions` — skeleton silhouette should match the new card (no tag bars, no install bar, single meta line).
+11. **Validate**: `bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun run test`.

lastPrompt: 3 or more columns is better

` block, the `visibleTags` / `hiddenTagCount` computeds, and the `tagLabel` + `TAG_VISIBLE_LIMIT` imports. Tag *filtering* is unaffected; cards just stop displaying them. The whole-card `NuxtLink` already targets the detail page (via the `after:absolute after:inset-0` pseudo-element); install lives on the detail page. Drop the `` markup and the `relative z-10` wrapper. - **Consolidate footer to a single bullet-separated meta line**: `🏢 Dept · ★ rating · ⬇ downloads`. All three on the same row, separated by middle dots, all in `text-[12px]` muted ink (rating + count stay font-mono for digit alignment). Remove the separate dept block above; dept now lives on the meta line. - **Spacing adjustment** — drop the card's internal `gap-3` to `gap-2` and `p-[18px]` to `p-4` so the now-shorter card doesn't feel padded. The card will end up roughly 280×130px on desktop instead of 280×220px — a natural wide rectangle. **File:** `app/components/extension/ExtGridSkeleton.vue` - Drop the tag-pill placeholder row and the install-button placeholder bar - Match new dimensions and spacing so the skeleton silhouette matches the simplified card **File:** `app/components/extension/ExtGrid.vue` - Keep the existing `grid-cols-[repeat(auto-fill,minmax(280px,1fr))] gap-4` — auto-fill at 280px gives 3–4 columns on a standard desktop, which the user has confirmed is the preferred density. The card's new rectangular shape comes from removing rows, not from changing column count. ### Commit 6 — `refactor(ext-list): quiet FilterBar to a single pill row` Visual goal: keep pills + popover triggers (the user opted for "quiet pills" over text-link rails), but lighten contrast, drop the prefix labels, and collapse to a single row. Border-bottom goes away entirely. **File:** `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` - Drop `border-b border-(--color-border)/60 pb-3` entirely; replace with `mb-6` whitespace only — no rule. - Collapse to a single `flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2` row containing: ` `. - TagDrawer's *trigger* now sits inline with the other controls; its *panel* (when open) still expands below the rail as it does today. - Remove the `` reference (deletion comes in commit 7). **File:** `app/components/filters/ScopePills.vue` - Lighten the active state: replace `bg-(--color-ink) text-(--color-bg) border-(--color-ink)` (full inversion) with `bg-(--color-ink)/8 text-(--color-ink) border-(--color-ink)/25 font-semibold` — a tinted, outlined pill rather than a filled one. - Keep inactive style as-is (`border-(--color-border) text-(--color-ink-muted)` with hover bump to ink). - Reduce padding from `px-3 py-1` to `px-2.5 py-0.5` so the row reads as a single line of compact pills. **Files:** `app/components/filters/DeptPicker.vue`, `app/components/filters/CreatorPicker.vue`, `app/components/filters/PublisherPicker.vue` - Drop the `{{ t("filters.dept.label") }}:` prefix from each trigger (was "Department:", "Creator:", "Publisher:"). The popover trigger now shows the category name *when no selection is active* (e.g., `Dept ▾`) or the selected value *when set* (e.g., `Eng / Cloud ▾`). - Reduce trigger padding to `px-2.5 py-0.5` and font to `text-[12px]` to match the new compact pill row. - Soften the active border from `border-(--color-ink)/35` to `border-(--color-ink)/20`. - Selected-state still uses bold text + ink color to mark "this filter is set" — the bold weight does the work the border used to. **File:** `app/components/filters/TagDrawer.vue` - Trigger button: match the new compact pill style (`px-2.5 py-0.5 text-[12px]`). - The expandable panel below stays unchanged (multi-select tag pills, match-mode toggle, clear-tags link all still work as-is). - Keep the accent-tinted count badge when tags are active — it's allowed by decision #11 (filter-active state). ### Commit 7 — `refactor(ext-list): drop FilterChips row` **Files:** - `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` — remove `` reference (already handled in commit 6's reshape; commit 7 finalizes the cleanup if any reference remains). - `app/components/filters/FilterChips.vue` — delete the file. Rationale: - "Trending" / "New" overlap with the sort dropdown (Stars / Downloads / Recent already cover the temporal axis). - "Official" is already visible as a gold badge on every official card — the filter chip is redundant with the visual signal. - "Open Source" / "Free" is rarely the differentiator for a tools marketplace; if it becomes one, add as a toggle in ResultsSummary later. The `filter` URL param still validates against the schema — old bookmarked URLs (`?filter=trending`) keep working and surface as a dismissible chip in ResultsSummary. We just stop *setting* it from the UI. i18n keys (`filters.chips.*`) are left in place — they cost nothing and the dismissible chip still uses them. ## Critical files **Edited:** - `app/components/extension/ExtCard.vue` — commit 5 - `app/components/extension/ExtGridSkeleton.vue` — commit 5 - `app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue` — commit 6 (and final cleanup in 7) - `app/components/filters/ScopePills.vue` — commit 6 - `app/components/filters/DeptPicker.vue` — commit 6 - `app/components/filters/CreatorPicker.vue` — commit 6 - `app/components/filters/PublisherPicker.vue` — commit 6 - `app/components/filters/TagDrawer.vue` — commit 6 **Deleted:** - `app/components/filters/FilterChips.vue` — commit 7 **Untouched (verified still needed):** - `app/pages/extensions/index.vue` — header + ResultsSummary + ExtGrid wiring stays as round 1 shipped - `app/components/extension/ResultsSummary.vue` — the dismissible `filter` chip stays in place for old-URL forward compat ## Reused utilities (round 2) - `formatCount()` and `deptPath()` / `MY_DEPT_ID` — all still used by ExtCard's simplified meta line - `useFilters()` — unchanged - CSS vars (`--color-ink`, `--color-ink-muted`, `--color-border`, `--color-accent`, `--color-card`, `--radius-card`) — unchanged ## Accent budget Post-round-2, the list page's accent moments are: - ✅ Card hover border tint (unchanged) - ✅ TagDrawer active filter count badge (unchanged) - ✅ "View Panorama" link (unchanged) - ✅ Dept "Mine" emphasis on cards (text-only accent, kept from round 1) - ❌ ResultsSummary chips: neutral - ❌ Scope pills: now ink-tinted active state, no accent **The InstallButton is gone from the list page** — install only exists on detail pages now. This removes the strongest accent footprint from the list. Net: significantly less accent ink on the page, which fits decision #11's "one intentional accent moment per viewport" goal. ## Verification 1. **Local dev**: `bun dev` → open `http://localhost:3000/en/extensions`. 2. **Card structure**: confirm cards show icon + name + badge on the header row, 2-line description, and a single meta line (`🏢 Dept · ★ N.N · ⬇ count`). No tag pills, no Install button on the card. 3. **Whole-card click**: clicking anywhere on a card body navigates to the detail page where InstallButton still exists. 4. **Filter bar**: confirm the rail is a single line of compact pills (Scope pills + Dept/Creator/Publisher/Tags triggers). No horizontal rule below. 5. **Tag drawer**: clicking "Tags ▾" still expands the multi-select panel; selecting tags still adds them as filters and dismissible chips appear in ResultsSummary. 6. **FilterChips gone**: no Trending/New/Official/Free pill row anywhere. URL `/en/extensions?filter=trending` still loads cleanly; the dismissible chip in ResultsSummary still appears (forward compat). 7. **Picker trigger labels**: when no selection, triggers show category name only (`Dept ▾`); when a selection is active, they show the selected value (`Eng / Cloud ▾`) in bold. 8. **Locale parity**: switch to `/zh/extensions` — card meta line and pickers render in Chinese. 9. **Theme parity**: Ivory → Dark → Mono Clean — all three should render the new lighter pill active state without darkness/contrast issues. Dark theme's lighter scope pill (`bg-ink/8`) may need an opacity bump (`/15` or `/20`) — adjust if needed during implementation. 10. **Skeleton**: hard-reload `/en/extensions` — skeleton silhouette should match the new card (no tag bars, no install bar, single meta line). 11. **Validate**: `bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun run test`. ## Out of scope - Pagination polish, infinite scroll - Density toggle (list vs. grid view) - Card SaveButton / favorites surfacing on hover - Filter persistence across sessions - Sidebar refinement - Detail page changes

new silhouette.
EOF

input.command:
git add app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue app/components/filters/ScopePills.vue app/components/filters/DeptPicker.vue app/components/filters/CreatorPicker.vue app/components/filters/PublisherPicker.vue app/components/filters/TagDrawer.vue && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
refactor(ext-list): quiet FilterBar to a single pill row

Drop the horizontal divider entirely — even at 60% opacity, it kept
breaking the editorial flow. Collapse the picker row, the tag drawer
trigger, and the soon-to-be-removed quick-filter row into a single flex
rail. Lighten ScopePills's active state from a full ink inversion to a
subtle tinted outline (bg-ink/8, border-ink/25) so the active filter
reads as set rather than asserted. Drop the "Department:/Creator:/Publisher:"
prefix labels from picker triggers — the active value (or category name
when empty) speaks for itself; the active selection also lives in the
dismissible-chip row below. Standardize all pills to the same compact
size (px-2.5 py-0.5 text-[12px]). TagDrawer's panel now wraps to a new
row below the rail via display:contents + basis-full, so the rail stays
single-row whether tags are expanded or not.
EOF
)"

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
refactor(ext-list): drop FilterChips component

The quick-filter pill row (All / Trending / New / Official / Open Source)
duplicates existing affordances: Trending and New overlap with the sort
dropdown's recency axis, Official is already legible as a gold badge on
the card itself, and Open Source rarely drives discovery in a tools
marketplace. With those four collapsed away, the filter rail reads as a
single quiet line of pills.

The schema's filter URL param still validates (?filter=trending continues
to parse cleanly), and ResultsSummary still renders a dismissible chip
for it — old bookmarked URLs keep working. We've just stopped setting it
from the UI.
EOF
)"

content.text:
All 7 tasks completed; no new tasks needed. Round 2 done.

Round 2 added three commits on `feat/ext-list-elegance`:

- **`refactor(ext-list): simplify ExtCard to a preview rectangle`** (724d183) — dropped the tag pills row and the inline InstallButton; consolidated dept/stars/downloads onto a single bullet-separated meta line; tightened padding/gap so the card reads as a wider-than-tall preview, not an action panel.
- **`refactor(ext-list): quiet FilterBar to a single pill row`** (93ba9be) — removed the horizontal divider entirely; collapsed all controls into one flex rail; lightened ScopePills's active state from full inversion to a tinted outline (`bg-ink/8`, `border-ink/25`); dropped the "Department:/Creator:/Publisher:" prefix labels so triggers show only the active value (or category name when empty); TagDrawer's expanded panel wraps to a new row via `display:contents + basis-full`.
- **`refactor(ext-list): drop FilterChips component`** (b938ebb) — removed the redundant All/Trending/New/Official/Open Source pill row and deleted the component. Old URLs with `?filter=trending` still parse and surface as dismissible chips in ResultsSummary.

Verified: lint clean, 250/250 tests pass, dev server returns 200, Install button absent from cards, no `#tag` pills on cards, no FilterChips row in markup, lightened pill classes (`bg-(--color-ink)/8`, `border-(--color-ink)/20`) confirmed in active-filter URLs.

Nothing pushed — `git push -u origin feat/ext-list-elegance` is yours to run when ready.

message.content: I think the cards should be bigger, and the internal padding needs to be larger. what do you think?

content.text:
I think you're right — after removing tags + install button, the cards may now feel cramped at `p-4` (16px). My read:

- **Padding**: bump `p-4` → `p-5` (20px) or `p-6` (24px). At `p-6` cards feel deliberately spacious.
- **Min card width**: keep ≥3 columns (you confirmed earlier). Going from `minmax(280px)` to `minmax(320–360px)` shifts most desktop layouts from 4 cards/row to 3 cards/row, so each card gets wider.
- **Internal gap**: bump `gap-2` (8px) → `gap-3` (12px) so the three rows breathe.
- **Description**: with more height available, could grow from 2 lines to 3 lines.

Let me confirm the dial before changing anything.

options.description: minmax(320px) — 3 cards per row on standard desktop, 4 on wider screens. Cards a bit wider than now but still dense.

options.label: Larger still

options.description: More room means longer descriptions can render fully without being truncated mid-sentence.

options.label: Keep 2 lines
options.description: Cards stay shorter; the extra height becomes pure breathing room.

content.text: Different page this time (detail vs. list). Per the re-entering instructions, I'll start fresh. Let me explore the detail page first.

input.prompt:
I'm planning an elegance pass on the extension detail page in a Nuxt 4 app at /Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter-nuxt.

The product is AgentCenter — a marketplace for AI agent extensions (Skills, MCP servers, slash commands, plugins). The detail page is the surface a user lands on after clicking an extension card on the browse list. From CLAUDE.md decision #6: detail page README is raw markdown stored, server-rendered with markdown-it + DOMPurify; manifest metadata (homepage, repo, license, compatibility, screenshots) renders as a side panel.

Please find and report on:

1. **The detail page route file** — likely `app/pages/extensions/[slug].vue` (locale-prefixed). Report its full path and the complete content (or close to it if long, with line counts and structural excerpts of any sections you abbreviate). I want to see how it composes child components and lays out the page.

2. **Detail-page components** — anything under `app/components/extension/` that's specific to detail (NOT list/browse). I expect at least:
   - `ExtTabs.vue` (the Overview/Setup/Permissions tab strip per i18n keys I've seen)
   - `InstallButton.vue` (also used on list but configured larger here)
   - `SaveButton.vue` (bookmark)
   - Possibly a side-panel component for manifest metadata
   - Possibly a related/recommended extensions block
   - Possibly a README renderer component
   - Possibly a permissions or setup-instructions component
   For each, file path + a 2–3 sentence description of what it renders.

3. **Server endpoint** feeding the detail page — likely under `server/api/internal/extensions/[slug].get.ts` or similar. Just the path and the response shape (Extension type) — no need to dump full SQL.

4. **README rendering** — where does the markdown-it + DOMPurify rendering happen? Server-side under `server/utils/`? A composable? Report the file path and the rough pipeline (input markdown → sanitized HTML).

5. **i18n keys** used on the detail page — the `extensions.*` section in `i18n/locales/en.json` for tabs, manifest labels (homepage, repository, license, scope, published, related, about), setup hint, permissions, copy/copied, share. Just list them so I know what's already translated.

6. **Visual hierarchy issues** — observations about the current layout/typography/density that stand out. Things like: fragmented font-size scale, repeated rendering of the same info, mismatch between the editorial Ivory aesthetic of the list page (which we just refined) and the detail page. Note where InstallButton is positioned, how the tabs read, whether the side panel feels balanced.

Report findings as a structured summary. Include file paths with line numbers for the most important code locations. Do NOT propose changes — just paint a clear picture of what exists today, plus any frictions you notice that might motivate refinements.

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary of my findings. ## Extension Detail Page Exploration Report I've conducted a thorough investigation of the extension detail page architecture in the AgentCenter Nuxt app. Here are my findings organized by the six categories you requested: --- ### 1. Detail Page Route File **Path:** `/Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter-nuxt/app/pages/extensions/[slug].vue` **Structure & Composition:** - 42 lines total - Fetches extension detail data from `/api/internal/extension-detail` (query-based, slug computed) - Handles 404 on missing extension (line 12-14) - Computes i18n-aware `name`, `description`, and `publishedAt` - Computes `shareUrl` for social sharing - Defines OG image with bundled Frame template - **Main layout (lines 45-79):** Two-column grid - `lg:grid-cols-[1fr_300px]` — main content (flexible) + 300px fixed sidebar - `gap-8` between columns - `px-6 py-8 max-w-7xl` outer container **Child components in sequence:** 1. **ExtHero** — Title, description, rating, downloads, department, action buttons (Install/Save/Share) 2. **ExtTabs** — Three-tab interface: Overview (readme), Setup (install command), Permissions 3. **ExtAboutCard** (sidebar) — License, scope, published date, homepage & repo links 4. **ExtRelatedList** (sidebar) — Recommended extensions with icons --- ### 2. Detail-Page Components | Component | Path | Purpose | |-----------|------|---------| | **ExtHero** | `app/components/extension/ExtHero.vue` | Hero header with icon (14px emoji in 56px container), h1 title (`font-serif text-3xl`), description, star/download badges, department trail, action button strip (66 lines) | | **ExtTabs** | `app/components/extension/ExtTabs.vue` | Tab strip (reka-ui Tabs) with three triggers: Overview/Setup/Permissions; Overview renders Markdown + tag pills; Setup shows install command; Permissions lists permission keys (75 lines) | | **InstallButton** | `app/components/extension/InstallButton.vue` | Primary CTA button; `text-[12px]` default or `text-[11px]` when `size="sm"`; dual-size support via computed `sizeClass`; states: default (accent bg), loading (opacity-60), installed (accent/10 bg + checkmark) (58 lines) | | **SaveButton** | `app/components/extension/SaveButton.vue` | Secondary bookmark button; two variants: `pill` (with label) and `icon`; on detail page used as `pill` (no variant prop); 3s toast feedback on save (58 lines) | | **ShareButton** | `app/components/extension/ShareButton.vue` | Uses Web Share API with fallback to clipboard; 1.4s toast on copy; bordered neutral style (48 lines) | | **ExtAboutCard** | `app/components/extension/ExtAboutCard.vue` | Side panel card (`rounded-(--radius-card) border bg-(--color-card) p-5`); h2 "About" header (`text-sm font-semibold`); dl with 3 items (license, scope, published); separator + links (homepage/repo) with icons (79 lines) | | **ExtRelatedList** | `app/components/extension/ExtRelatedList.vue` | Side panel card (same styling as ExtAboutCard); h2 "Related" header; ul of NuxtLinks with emoji + name + truncate (40 lines) | | **Markdown** | `app/components/Markdown.vue` | README renderer; markdown-it + DOMPurify sanitization; renders to `prose prose-sm max-w-none` div; allows h1-h6, p, lists, code/pre, tables, links, img, blockquote; strips class attrs but allows href/src/alt (31 lines) | | **InstallCommand** | `app/components/extension/InstallCommand.vue` | Code block in terminal dark theme (`bg-(--color-ink) text-(--color-bg) p-3`); shows `$ agentcenter install {slug}`; copy button with feedback (38 lines) | --- ### 3. Server Endpoint & Response Shape **Path:** `/Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter-nuxt/server/api/internal/extension-detail.get.ts` **Response shape:** ```typescript { ext: Extension, // Main extension record versions: Version[], // Version history (fetched but not used on detail page currently) related: RelatedRow[] // 3-5 recommended extensions with id/slug/name/nameZh/iconEmoji/iconColor } ``` **Data flow:** - Query param: `slug` (string, required) - Queries: - `extensionsRepo.findBySlug(db, slug)` → full extension - `versionsRepo.listForExtension(db, ext.id)` → versions - `extensionsRepo.findRelated(db, ext.id, ext.category)` → related by category - Error handling: 404 if not found, 400 if slug missing **Extension properties accessed on detail page:** - Identity: `id`, `slug` - Editorial: `name`, `nameZh`, `description`, `descriptionZh`, `iconEmoji`, `iconColor` - Metadata: `licenseSpdx`, `scope`, `publishedAt`, `homepageUrl`, `repoUrl`, `deptId` - Content: `readmeMd` (markdown string, server-rendered on detail page) - Tags: `tagIds` (array of string IDs, used in tags section of Overview tab) - Permissions: `permissions` (object, keys indicate true permissions; null if none) - Stats: `starsAvg` (string, avg rating), `downloadsCount` (number) --- ### 4. README Rendering Pipeline **Markdown Component:** `/Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter-nuxt/app/components/Markdown.vue` **Pipeline:** 1. **Input:** `source` prop (raw markdown string from `ext.readmeMd`) 2. **Parse:** `MarkdownIt` instance (lines 7-12) - `html: false` — no raw HTML pass-through - `linkify: true` — auto-link URLs - `breaks: false` — no `\n` → `
` conversion - `typographer: true` — smart quotes/dashes 3. **Render:** `md.render(source)` → raw HTML 4. **Sanitize:** `DOMPurify.sanitize()` with allowlist (lines 16-24) - **Allowed tags:** h1–h6, p, a, ul, ol, li, strong, em, code, pre, blockquote, hr, br, img, table (thead/tbody/tr/th/td), span, div - **Allowed attributes:** href, title, alt, src, class - **URI whitelist:** https://, http://, mailto:, relative (/, ../) 5. **Output:** `v-html` into `
` **Location:** Browser-side (Markdown.vue is an app component, rendering happens client-side). Note: CLAUDE.md §6 says "server-rendered with markdown-it + DOMPurify" but implementation is client-side. The markdown string itself is fetched as-is from the server; rendering happens in the browser. --- ### 5. i18n Keys for Detail Page From `/Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter-nuxt/i18n/locales/en.json`, the `extensions` object includes: **Detail-page specific:** ``` extensions.tabs.overview "Overview" extensions.tabs.setup "Setup" extensions.tabs.permissions "Permissions" extensions.noReadme "No README has been published..." extensions.setupHint "Run this in your terminal..." extensions.noPermissions "This extension doesn't request..." extensions.about "About" extensions.license "License" extensions.scope "Scope" extensions.published "Published" extensions.homepage "Homepage" extensions.repository "Repository" extensions.related "Related" extensions.copy "Copy" extensions.copied "Copied" extensions.share "Share" ``` **Shared (common):** ``` common.install "Install" common.installing "Installing…" common.installed "Installed" common.save "Save" common.saved "Saved" ``` **Permissions** (dynamic per permission key): ``` publish.wizard.listing.permissions.{key} [fallback to key itself if missing] ``` --- ### 6. Visual Hierarchy & Frictions Observed #### **Font & Spacing Consistency:** - **Hero title:** `font-serif text-3xl` (Fraunces, largest on page) - **Card headers:** `text-sm font-semibold` (ExtAboutCard, ExtRelatedList h2) - **Body text:** Mixed: `text-sm` (tabs), `text-[13px]` (ExtCard description), `text-[12px]` (stats), `text-[11px]` (tag pills) - **Friction:** No unified body size scale. List page uses `text-[15px]` for card titles (ExtCard), `text-[13px]` for description. Detail hero uses `font-serif text-3xl`. Sidebar uses `text-sm` everywhere. #### **Layout & Density:** - **Main container:** `px-6 py-8 max-w-7xl` — matches browse page - **Hero spacing:** Hero is `mb-6` from ExtTabs, which starts `mt-4` in TabsContent (only 10px gap between sections) - **Sidebar:** Fixed 300px width, `gap-8` from main. Both cards in sidebar use `p-5` interior padding. - **Friction:** Sidebar panels (ExtAboutCard, ExtRelatedList) feel dense; `p-5` + `text-sm` + `space-y-2` is tight. Compare to ExtCard which has `p-5` but slightly more breathing room with larger font sizes. #### **Accent Usage:** - **Hero hero buttons:** InstallButton is `bg-(--color-accent) text-(--color-accent-fg)` — CORRECT per decision #11 (primary CTA) - **SaveButton:** `border-(--color-border) bg-(--color-card)` default, shifts to `border-(--color-accent) bg-(--color-accent)/10` when saved — CORRECT (secondary, accent only on active state) - **ShareButton:** Neutral `border-(--color-border)` throughout — CORRECT (tertiary) - **ExtAboutCard links:** `hover:text-(--color-accent)` — CORRECT (hover accent) - **Friction:** None here; restraint is well-maintained. #### **Mismatch: List vs. Detail Editorial Feel:** - **List page (ExtCard):** - Card background: white (`bg-(--color-card)`) - Border: subtle `border-(--color-border)` - Hover: `-translate-y-0.5` + shadow + `border-(--color-accent)/30` - Icon: 44px box, `text-[18px]` emoji - Title: `text-[15px] font-semibold` (compact, sans-serif) - Description: `text-[13px]` with `-webkit-line-clamp: 3` - Stats: `text-[12px] font-mono` - **Detail page (ExtHero + sidebar):** - Hero icon: 56px box, `text-[24px]` emoji - Hero title: `font-serif text-3xl` (serif, editorial) - Hero description: `text-(--color-ink-muted)` (secondary color, large) - Hero stats: `text-sm` (larger than list) - Sidebar: white cards with `p-5`, `text-sm` body - Overall: More editorial, serif-heavy, spacious hero; compact sidebar - **Friction:** The detail page **hero feels editorial and generous** (serif title, large emoji), but the **sidebar panels revert to utilitarian** (`text-sm`, dense `dl`, no visual breathing room). The prose README section uses default `prose` styling which may not match the Ivory aesthetic. No branded border or accent treatment on the readme container. #### **Button Positioning & Visual Weight:** - **Action buttons (Install/Save/Share):** Placed directly under hero in 3-button row, above tabs (line 59-63 in ExtHero) - **Friction:** Buttons are `text-[12px]` and `px-3 py-1.5`, making them **visual weight-neutral** (not prominent). The Install button is accent-colored, so it stands out, but the trio together reads as secondary compared to the large hero title. No clear visual hierarchy suggesting "Install this now" before reading the README. Compare to retail/SaaS patterns where primary CTAs are larger and more positioned. #### **Tab Styling:** - **TabsList:** `inline-flex h-9 items-center justify-center gap-1 rounded-md border border-(--color-border) bg-(--color-sidebar) p-1` - **TabsTrigger active:** `bg-(--color-card) text-(--color-ink) shadow-sm` - **Friction:** Tabs use `bg-(--color-sidebar)` as the container background, which is slightly darker than white. When a tab is active, it "pops" to white. This works but feels subtle; the color change is ~1.5% lightness shift (97.5% → 98.5%). No font-weight change on active, just color + shadow. #### **Related Extensions Block:** - **Styling:** Simple NuxtLink list with emoji + name, `hover:text-(--color-accent)`, `truncate` on name - **Friction:** No visual distinction between related items (no cards, no icons, no metadata). Reads as plain list compared to the feature-rich ExtCard on the browse page. Could feel like an afterthought. #### **Missing Visual Patterns:** - **README container:** No border, no background tint, no distinction from white background. Just `prose` + color. Could integrate better with the Ivory aesthetic if it had a subtle card or border. - **Breadcrumb:** No breadcrumb or back-link visible on detail page; if user lands from off-site, navigation back to browse is not obvious at first glance. - **Copy/Share feedback:** Transient toast messages (1.4–3s) are not visible; no UI indication success feedback was registered (common UX friction). #### **Typography Metrics Summary:** | Element | Detail Page | List Page | |---------|------------|-----------| | Primary title | `font-serif text-3xl` | `text-[15px] font-semibold` | | Description | `text-base` implicit | `text-[13px]` | | Stats | `text-sm` | `text-[12px] font-mono` | | Sidebar headers | `text-sm font-semibold` | N/A | | Body/dl | `text-sm` | N/A | | Tags | `text-[11px] font-semibold` | N/A | **Unified scale recommendation would be:** Consider a defined scale (e.g., `text-xs: 11px, text-sm: 13px, text-base: 15px, text-lg: 18px, text-xl: 24px, text-3xl: 32px`) and ensure detail sidebar uses `text-base` or `text-sm` consistently, not a mix with custom `text-[Xpx]` values. --- ### Summary of Key File Locations | Purpose | Path | Lines | |---------|------|-------| | Detail page route | `app/pages/extensions/[slug].vue` | 80 | | Hero section | `app/components/extension/ExtHero.vue` | 66 | | Tab strip | `app/components/extension/ExtTabs.vue` | 75 | | Sidebar about card | `app/components/extension/ExtAboutCard.vue` | 79 | | Related extensions | `app/components/extension/ExtRelatedList.vue` | 40 | | Install button | `app/components/extension/InstallButton.vue` | 58 | | Markdown renderer | `app/components/Markdown.vue` | 31 | | Server endpoint | `server/api/internal/extension-detail.get.ts` | 19 | | Theme variables | `app/assets/css/tailwind.css` | 91 | | i18n keys | `i18n/locales/en.json` | (excerpt) | This structure is well-modularized and clear, with coherent data flow from server → page → child components. The main elegance opportunity is in harmonizing typography scales, tightening the visual relationship between hero and sidebar, and giving the README content stronger editorial treatment to match the serif-heavy, spacious hero aesthetic.

I'm planning an elegance pass on the extension detail page in a Nuxt 4 app at /Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter-nuxt.

The product is AgentCenter — a marketplace for AI agent extensions (Skills, MCP servers, slash commands, plugins). The detail page is the surface a user lands on after clicking an extension card on the browse list. From CLAUDE.md decision #6: detail page README is raw markdown stored, server-rendered with markdown-it + DOMPurify; manifest metadata (homepage, repo, license, compatibility, screenshots) renders as a side panel.

Please find and report on:

1. **The detail page route file** — likely `app/pages/extensions/[slug].vue` (locale-prefixed). Report its full path and the complete content (or close to it if long, with line counts and structural excerpts of any sections you abbreviate). I want to see how it composes child components and lays out the page.

2. **Detail-page components** — anything under `app/components/extension/` that's specific to detail (NOT list/browse). I expect at least:
   - `ExtTabs.vue` (the Overview/Setup/Permissions tab strip per i18n keys I've seen)
   - `InstallButton.vue` (also used on list but configured larger here)
   - `SaveButton.vue` (bookmark)
   - Possibly a side-panel component for manifest metadata
   - Possibly a related/recommended extensions block
   - Possibly a README renderer component
   - Possibly a permissions or setup-instructions component
   For each, file path + a 2–3 sentence description of what it renders.

3. **Server endpoint** feeding the detail page — likely under `server/api/internal/extensions/[slug].get.ts` or similar. Just the path and the response shape (Extension type) — no need to dump full SQL.

4. **README rendering** — where does the markdown-it + DOMPurify rendering happen? Server-side under `server/utils/`? A composable? Report the file path and the rough pipeline (input markdown → sanitized HTML).

5. **i18n keys** used on the detail page — the `extensions.*` section in `i18n/locales/en.json` for tabs, manifest labels (homepage, repository, license, scope, published, related, about), setup hint, permissions, copy/copied, share. Just list them so I know what's already translated.

6. **Visual hierarchy issues** — observations about the current layout/typography/density that stand out. Things like: fragmented font-size scale, repeated rendering of the same info, mismatch between the editorial Ivory aesthetic of the list page (which we just refined) and the detail page. Note where InstallButton is positioned, how the tabs read, whether the side panel feels balanced.

Report findings as a structured summary. Include file paths with line numbers for the most important code locations. Do NOT propose changes — just paint a clear picture of what exists today, plus any frictions you notice that might motivate refinements.

content.text: I have enough now. Here's what stands out as elegance gaps: - **Hero CTA is undersized** — Install/Save/Share are `text-[12px] px-3 py-1.5` (same as on the old list cards). For the page's primary action, they read as secondary. The title is editorial (`font-serif text-3xl`); the buttons aren't. - **README sits naked** — The main content has no card, no border, no editorial framing. It just floats on the page background. Compare to the now-framed sidebar cards. - **Sidebar h2s read as spec-sheet** — "About" and "Related" use `text-sm font-semibold` plus dense `dl space-y-2`. With the hero in serif, the sidebar reverts to utilitarian. - **Tabs are timid** — Active state is `bg-(--color-sidebar)` → `bg-(--color-card)` (a ~1.5% lightness swap). The active tab is barely distinguishable. - **Related list is anemic** — Plain text-with-emoji list. No badge, no dept, no stars. Reads as afterthought. - **Stats line uses ASCII `·`** — Hardcoded period before dept trail (`· {{ deptTrail }}`), inconsistent with the proper middle-dot separators the new list card uses. - **No breadcrumb back to browse** — Users have to use browser back to return to the list. Let me ask which to tackle.

file.content:
<script setup lang="ts">
import MarkdownIt from "markdown-it"
import DOMPurify from "isomorphic-dompurify"

const props = defineProps<{ source: string }>()

const md = new MarkdownIt({
  html: false,
  linkify: true,
  breaks: false,
  typographer: true,
})

const rendered = computed(() => {
  const raw = md.render(props.source ?? "")
  return DOMPurify.sanitize(raw, {
    ALLOWED_TAGS: [
      "h1","h2","h3","h4","h5","h6","p","a","ul","ol","li","strong","em","code","pre",
      "blockquote","hr","br","img","table","thead","tbody","tr","th","td","span","div",
    ],
    ALLOWED_ATTR: ["href","title","alt","src","class"],
    ALLOWED_URI_REGEXP: /^(?:https?:|mailto:|\/|\.\.?\/)/i,
  })
})
</script>

<template>
  <!-- eslint-disable-next-line vue/no-v-html -- sanitized via DOMPurify above -->
  <div class="prose prose-sm max-w-none text-(--color-ink)" v-html="rendered" />
</template>

export async function findRelated(
  db: Transactable,
  extensionId: string,
  category: "skills" | "mcp" | "slash" | "plugins",
) {
  return db
    .select(relatedSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .where(
      and(
        eq(extensions.category, category),
        eq(extensions.visibility, "published"),
        ne(extensions.id, extensionId),
      ),
    )
    .orderBy(desc(extensions.downloadsCount))
    .limit(4)
}

export async function findBySlug(
  db: Transactable,
  slug: string,
  opts: { onlyPublished?: boolean } = {},
) {
  const onlyPublished = opts.onlyPublished ?? true
  const where = onlyPublished
    ? and(eq(extensions.slug, slug), eq(extensions.visibility, "published"))
    : eq(extensions.slug, slug)

  const [row] = await db
    .select(detailSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(where)
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
  return row ?? null
}

export async function findById(db: Transactable, id: string) {
  const [row] = await db
    .select(detailSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(eq(extensions.id, id))
