input.content:
# Extension detail page — elegance pass ## Context The list page has now had two rounds of refinement on this branch (`feat/ext-list-elegance`, 8 commits shipped: 9adb8be..e01ef18 + 368f0b7 gitignore). The detail page (`/en/extensions/[slug]`) hasn't been touched, and after the list-page work it now reads as mismatched: the hero is editorial-serif but the action buttons are tiny, the README sits naked on the background without framing, the sidebar cards revert to spec-sheet typography, tabs are barely distinguishable when active, and the Related list is anemic. Goal: bring the detail page in line with the editorial voice of the refined list page. Same branch, same PR — several more commits on top of the existing eight. ## Approach Five commits. ### Commit 9 — `feat(ext-detail): amplify hero with breadcrumb, lede, and size=lg CTA` **File:** `app/components/extension/InstallButton.vue` - Add a `size="lg"` variant alongside the existing `sm` and default: `px-4 py-2 text-[14px] gap-1.5`. Touch only the `sizeClass` computed and (optionally) the icon size used inside. **File:** `app/components/extension/ExtHero.vue` - **Title**: `font-serif text-3xl` → `font-serif text-4xl` for stronger anchor on the editorial page. - **Description**: bare paragraph → lede: `text-[17px] leading-[1.55] text-(--color-ink-muted) mt-3`. - **Icon**: `size-14 text-[24px]` → `size-16 text-[28px]`, matching the larger title. - **Stats line**: replace the hardcoded `· ` prefix on the dept span with proper middle-dot separators between every chip — same pattern the new list card uses (`·` between items). Use a single `text-sm` row. - **Action row**: pass `size="lg"` to InstallButton; bump SaveButton + ShareButton to matching `px-3.5 py-2 text-[13px]` so the trio reads as a confident CTA group. **File:** `app/pages/extensions/[slug].vue` - Add a subtle breadcrumb above the hero: `← Browse extensions`. - Use `useLocalePath` so the link respects the active locale. **Files:** `i18n/locales/en.json`, `i18n/locales/zh.json` - Add `extensions.backToBrowse`: "← Browse extensions" / "← 浏览扩展". ### Commit 10 — `refactor(ext-detail): frame the README and tab content in a card` **File:** `app/components/extension/ExtTabs.vue` - Wrap each `` body in a card div: `rounded-(--radius-card) border border-(--color-border) bg-(--color-card) p-6 mt-4`. The Overview tab gets the framing it's been missing; Setup and Permissions get matching containers so all three tab states have the same visual weight. - Drop the per-element `mt-4` / `mb-2` classes that exist to fake spacing — the card padding handles it now. - The tag pills row at the bottom of Overview (`
`) moves inside the same card, with a top divider: `border-t border-(--color-border) pt-4 mt-6`. **File:** `app/components/Markdown.vue` - Bump prose scale from `prose prose-sm` to `prose prose-sm` (unchanged) but adjust the wrapper so it works inside a `p-6` card without double-spacing. No structural change; just verify prose first-child top-margin is stripped — it already is via tailwind-typography defaults. ### Commit 11 — `refactor(ext-detail): editorial sidebar pass` **File:** `app/components/extension/ExtAboutCard.vue` - `h2`: `text-sm font-semibold` → `font-serif text-base font-semibold tracking-tight` (echoes the hero's serif voice in a smaller register). - Card padding: `p-5` → `p-6`. - `dl` spacing: `space-y-2 text-sm` → `space-y-2.5 text-[13px]`. - Links section: bump separator padding from `mt-4 pt-4` → `mt-5 pt-5`; link text size to `text-[13px]`. **File:** `app/components/extension/ExtRelatedList.vue` - Match the same serif h2 + p-6 + space-y-3 pattern. - Each `
  • ` row: enlarge emoji to `text-[18px]` and switch to `flex items-center gap-3` with `py-1` per row for vertical rhythm. ### Commit 12 — `refactor(ext-detail): underline-on-active tabs` **Files:** `app/components/extension/ExtTabs.vue` plus the underlying primitives if they need adjustment (`app/components/ui/Tabs.vue` / `TabsList.vue` / `TabsTrigger.vue` — check if shadcn-vue exposes these as overridable). - Drop the `bg-(--color-sidebar)` container and the `bg-(--color-card) shadow-sm` active swap. The current 1.5% lightness contrast is too timid. - New design: `TabsList` becomes a row of triggers with `border-b border-(--color-border)` below. Each trigger is `px-3 py-2 text-[14px] font-medium text-(--color-ink-muted) -mb-px border-b-2 border-transparent`. - Active trigger: `text-(--color-ink) font-semibold border-(--color-ink)` — the `border-b-2` overlapping the list's `border-b` produces a clean underline. - Hover (inactive): `text-(--color-ink)` (no border change). - Caveat: if shadcn-vue's Tabs primitives don't expose `class` overrides per trigger, fall back to wrapping with class bindings inside `ExtTabs.vue` only. ### Commit 13 — `feat(ext-detail): enrich Related list with badge and dept` **File:** `server/repositories/extensions.ts` - Extend `relatedSelect` (line 81) to include three more columns: `badge: extensions.badge`, `deptId: extensions.deptId`, `starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg`. No SQL change — just adding to the select projection. `downloadsCount` is already there. **File:** `app/components/extension/ExtRelatedList.vue` - Update the `RelatedRow` interface to match the enriched shape (add `badge`, `deptId`, `starsAvg`). - Each row becomes a tiny mini-card: emoji + name on row 1, `dept · ★ rating` muted meta on row 2. Optional badge pill inline with the name. - Keep the row clickable as a whole via the `NuxtLink`. ## Files modified **Edited:** - `app/pages/extensions/[slug].vue` — commit 9 (breadcrumb) - `app/components/extension/ExtHero.vue` — commit 9 - `app/components/extension/InstallButton.vue` — commit 9 - `app/components/extension/ExtTabs.vue` — commits 10, 12 - `app/components/extension/ExtAboutCard.vue` — commit 11 - `app/components/extension/ExtRelatedList.vue` — commits 11, 13 - `server/repositories/extensions.ts` — commit 13 - `i18n/locales/en.json`, `i18n/locales/zh.json` — commit 9 **Possibly edited (if Tabs primitives need override):** - `app/components/ui/TabsList.vue`, `app/components/ui/TabsTrigger.vue` — commit 12 (only if shadcn-vue Tabs primitives can't accept custom classes from the parent) **Untouched (verified still right):** - `app/components/extension/SaveButton.vue` — size bump applied via class only, no component change needed - `app/components/extension/ShareButton.vue` — same - `app/components/extension/InstallCommand.vue` — keeps current terminal look; lives inside the new framed Setup tab - `app/components/Markdown.vue` — keeps current prose render; the new card frame is in ExtTabs ## Reused utilities - `useLocalePath()` — for the breadcrumb's locale-aware link - `deptPath()` from `shared/data/departments.ts` — for the new Related row meta - CSS vars (`--color-ink`, `--color-ink-muted`, `--color-border`, `--color-accent`, `--color-card`, `--radius-card`) — unchanged - `badge-official` / `badge-popular` / `badge-new` classes from `tailwind.css` — reused in Related rows - Existing `Tabs` primitive set (`Tabs`, `TabsList`, `TabsTrigger`, `TabsContent`) — kept unless commit 12 forces a primitive override ## Accent budget (decision #11) The detail page is one of the two surfaces explicitly allowed an accent CTA. After this pass: - ✅ Install button: now `size="lg"` accent CTA — the page's intentional accent moment, more prominent - ✅ SaveButton active-state border + bg (existing) - ✅ Permissions list dots (existing accent dots) - ✅ "Mine" dept emphasis (existing) - ❌ Tabs active state: now ink underline, not accent — keeps the budget tight The hero now carries one bold accent CTA instead of three muted same-size buttons, which fits the "one intentional accent moment" goal better than today. ## Verification 1. **Local dev**: `bun dev` → open `http://localhost:3001/en/extensions/`. 2. **Breadcrumb**: a `← Browse extensions` link sits above the hero; clicking returns to `/en/extensions`. 3. **Hero**: title reads at `text-4xl` serif; description is a noticeably larger lede; emoji is size-16; stats row uses middle-dot separators throughout. 4. **CTA group**: Install reads as the primary action (larger, accent fill); Save + Share match in size but stay neutral. 5. **README card**: Overview tab content lives inside a card with border + p-6; tags appear at the card's bottom with a divider above them; Setup and Permissions tabs use the same card frame. 6. **Sidebar**: "About" and "Related" headers are serif text-base; cards have p-6 breathing room; Related rows show `dept · ★ rating` under the name. 7. **Tabs**: active tab has a clean ink underline; inactive triggers are muted ink that goes ink on hover; the `bg-(--color-sidebar)` container is gone. 8. **Locale parity**: switch to `/zh/extensions/` — breadcrumb and all new copy renders in Chinese. 9. **Theme parity**: cycle Ivory → Dark → Mono Clean. Card frames around tab content should be visible in all three; tab underline should remain legible in Dark (ink is bright in dark mode). 10. **Validate**: `bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun run test`. ## Out of scope - Versions panel / version history surface - Screenshots gallery - Inline comments / reviews - Detail-page error and loading skeletons (separate concern from the list skeleton) - TopBar / Sidebar changes - Pagination / discovery beyond the Related sidebar ## Prior work on this branch (reference) - C1 `9adb8be` refactor(ext-list): tighten ExtCard hierarchy and typography - C2 `cff0aea` feat(ext-list): add ResultsSummary with active filter chips - C3 `3fee07a` refactor(ext-list): relocate sort to ResultsSummary, soften FilterBar - C4 `08a1b5c` feat(ext-list): add ExtGridSkeleton for first paint - C5 `724d183` refactor(ext-list): simplify ExtCard to a preview rectangle - C6 `93ba9be` refactor(ext-list): quiet FilterBar to a single pill row - C7 `b938ebb` refactor(ext-list): drop FilterChips component - C8 `e01ef18` refactor(ext-list): roomier ExtCard and grid - (also `368f0b7` chore(entire): ignore redactors/local/)

Use a single `text-sm` row. - **Action row**: pass `size="lg"` to InstallButton; bump SaveButton + ShareButton to matching `px-3.5 py-2 text-[13px]` so the trio reads as a confident CTA group. **File:** `app/pages/extensions/[slug].vue` - Add a subtle breadcrumb above the hero: `← Browse extensions`. - Use `useLocalePath` so the link respects the active locale. **Files:** `i18n/locales/en.json`, `i18n/locales/zh.json` - Add `extensions.backToBrowse`: "← Browse extensions" / "← 浏览扩展". ### Commit 10 — `refactor(ext-detail): frame the README and tab content in a card` **File:** `app/components/extension/ExtTabs.vue` - Wrap each `` body in a card div: `rounded-(--radius-card) border border-(--color-border) bg-(--color-card) p-6 mt-4`. The Overview tab gets the framing it's been missing; Setup and Permissions get matching containers so all three tab states have the same visual weight. - Drop the per-element `mt-4` / `mb-2` classes that exist to fake spacing — the card padding handles it now. - The tag pills row at the bottom of Overview (`
`) moves inside the same card, with a top divider: `border-t border-(--color-border) pt-4 mt-6`. **File:** `app/components/Markdown.vue` - Bump prose scale from `prose prose-sm` to `prose prose-sm` (unchanged) but adjust the wrapper so it works inside a `p-6` card without double-spacing. No structural change; just verify prose first-child top-margin is stripped — it already is via tailwind-typography defaults. ### Commit 11 — `refactor(ext-detail): editorial sidebar pass` **File:** `app/components/extension/ExtAboutCard.vue` - `h2`: `text-sm font-semibold` → `font-serif text-base font-semibold tracking-tight` (echoes the hero's serif voice in a smaller register). - Card padding: `p-5` → `p-6`. - `dl` spacing: `space-y-2 text-sm` → `space-y-2.5 text-[13px]`. - Links section: bump separator padding from `mt-4 pt-4` → `mt-5 pt-5`; link text size to `text-[13px]`. **File:** `app/components/extension/ExtRelatedList.vue` - Match the same serif h2 + p-6 + space-y-3 pattern. - Each `
  • ` row: enlarge emoji to `text-[18px]` and switch to `flex items-center gap-3` with `py-1` per row for vertical rhythm. ### Commit 12 — `refactor(ext-detail): underline-on-active tabs` **Files:** `app/components/extension/ExtTabs.vue` plus the underlying primitives if they need adjustment (`app/components/ui/Tabs.vue` / `TabsList.vue` / `TabsTrigger.vue` — check if shadcn-vue exposes these as overridable). - Drop the `bg-(--color-sidebar)` container and the `bg-(--color-card) shadow-sm` active swap. The current 1.5% lightness contrast is too timid. - New design: `TabsList` becomes a row of triggers with `border-b border-(--color-border)` below. Each trigger is `px-3 py-2 text-[14px] font-medium text-(--color-ink-muted) -mb-px border-b-2 border-transparent`. - Active trigger: `text-(--color-ink) font-semibold border-(--color-ink)` — the `border-b-2` overlapping the list's `border-b` produces a clean underline. - Hover (inactive): `text-(--color-ink)` (no border change). - Caveat: if shadcn-vue's Tabs primitives don't expose `class` overrides per trigger, fall back to wrapping with class bindings inside `ExtTabs.vue` only. ### Commit 13 — `feat(ext-detail): enrich Related list with badge and dept` **File:** `server/repositories/extensions.ts` - Extend `relatedSelect` (line 81) to include three more columns: `badge: extensions.badge`, `deptId: extensions.deptId`, `starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg`. No SQL change — just adding to the select projection. `downloadsCount` is already there. **File:** `app/components/extension/ExtRelatedList.vue` - Update the `RelatedRow` interface to match the enriched shape (add `badge`, `deptId`, `starsAvg`). - Each row becomes a tiny mini-card: emoji + name on row 1, `dept · ★ rating` muted meta on row 2. Optional badge pill inline with the name. - Keep the row clickable as a whole via the `NuxtLink`. ## Files modified **Edited:** - `app/pages/extensions/[slug].vue` — commit 9 (breadcrumb) - `app/components/extension/ExtHero.vue` — commit 9 - `app/components/extension/InstallButton.vue` — commit 9 - `app/components/extension/ExtTabs.vue` — commits 10, 12 - `app/components/extension/ExtAboutCard.vue` — commit 11 - `app/components/extension/ExtRelatedList.vue` — commits 11, 13 - `server/repositories/extensions.ts` — commit 13 - `i18n/locales/en.json`, `i18n/locales/zh.json` — commit 9 **Possibly edited (if Tabs primitives need override):** - `app/components/ui/TabsList.vue`, `app/components/ui/TabsTrigger.vue` — commit 12 (only if shadcn-vue Tabs primitives can't accept custom classes from the parent) **Untouched (verified still right):** - `app/components/extension/SaveButton.vue` — size bump applied via class only, no component change needed - `app/components/extension/ShareButton.vue` — same - `app/components/extension/InstallCommand.vue` — keeps current terminal look; lives inside the new framed Setup tab - `app/components/Markdown.vue` — keeps current prose render; the new card frame is in ExtTabs ## Reused utilities - `useLocalePath()` — for the breadcrumb's locale-aware link - `deptPath()` from `shared/data/departments.ts` — for the new Related row meta - CSS vars (`--color-ink`, `--color-ink-muted`, `--color-border`, `--color-accent`, `--color-card`, `--radius-card`) — unchanged - `badge-official` / `badge-popular` / `badge-new` classes from `tailwind.css` — reused in Related rows - Existing `Tabs` primitive set (`Tabs`, `TabsList`, `TabsTrigger`, `TabsContent`) — kept unless commit 12 forces a primitive override ## Accent budget (decision #11) The detail page is one of the two surfaces explicitly allowed an accent CTA. After this pass: - ✅ Install button: now `size="lg"` accent CTA — the page's intentional accent moment, more prominent - ✅ SaveButton active-state border + bg (existing) - ✅ Permissions list dots (existing accent dots) - ✅ "Mine" dept emphasis (existing) - ❌ Tabs active state: now ink underline, not accent — keeps the budget tight The hero now carries one bold accent CTA instead of three muted same-size buttons, which fits the "one intentional accent moment" goal better than today. ## Verification 1. **Local dev**: `bun dev` → open `http://localhost:3001/en/extensions/`. 2. **Breadcrumb**: a `← Browse extensions` link sits above the hero; clicking returns to `/en/extensions`. 3. **Hero**: title reads at `text-4xl` serif; description is a noticeably larger lede; emoji is size-16; stats row uses middle-dot separators throughout. 4. **CTA group**: Install reads as the primary action (larger, accent fill); Save + Share match in size but stay neutral. 5. **README card**: Overview tab content lives inside a card with border + p-6; tags appear at the card's bottom with a divider above them; Setup and Permissions tabs use the same card frame. 6. **Sidebar**: "About" and "Related" headers are serif text-base; cards have p-6 breathing room; Related rows show `dept · ★ rating` under the name. 7. **Tabs**: active tab has a clean ink underline; inactive triggers are muted ink that goes ink on hover; the `bg-(--color-sidebar)` container is gone. 8. **Locale parity**: switch to `/zh/extensions/` — breadcrumb and all new copy renders in Chinese. 9. **Theme parity**: cycle Ivory → Dark → Mono Clean. Card frames around tab content should be visible in all three; tab underline should remain legible in Dark (ink is bright in dark mode). 10. **Validate**: `bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun run test`. ## Out of scope - Versions panel / version history surface - Screenshots gallery - Inline comments / reviews - Detail-page error and loading skeletons (separate concern from the list skeleton) - TopBar / Sidebar changes - Pagination / discovery beyond the Related sidebar ## Prior work on this branch (reference) - C1 `9adb8be` refactor(ext-list): tighten ExtCard hierarchy and typography - C2 `cff0aea` feat(ext-list): add ResultsSummary with active filter chips - C3 `3fee07a` refactor(ext-list): relocate sort to ResultsSummary, soften FilterBar - C4 `08a1b5c` feat(ext-list): add ExtGridSkeleton for first paint - C5 `724d183` refactor(ext-list): simplify ExtCard to a preview rectangle - C6 `93ba9be` refactor(ext-list): quiet FilterBar to a single pill row - C7 `b938ebb` refactor(ext-list): drop FilterChips component - C8 `e01ef18` refactor(ext-list): roomier ExtCard and grid - (also `368f0b7` chore(entire): ignore redactors/local/)

After this pass: - ✅ Install button: now `size="lg"` accent CTA — the page's intentional accent moment, more prominent - ✅ SaveButton active-state border + bg (existing) - ✅ Permissions list dots (existing accent dots) - ✅ "Mine" dept emphasis (existing) - ❌ Tabs active state: now ink underline, not accent — keeps the budget tight The hero now carries one bold accent CTA instead of three muted same-size buttons, which fits the "one intentional accent moment" goal better than today. ## Verification 1. **Local dev**: `bun dev` → open `http://localhost:3001/en/extensions/`. 2. **Breadcrumb**: a `← Browse extensions` link sits above the hero; clicking returns to `/en/extensions`. 3. **Hero**: title reads at `text-4xl` serif; description is a noticeably larger lede; emoji is size-16; stats row uses middle-dot separators throughout. 4. **CTA group**: Install reads as the primary action (larger, accent fill); Save + Share match in size but stay neutral. 5. **README card**: Overview tab content lives inside a card with border + p-6; tags appear at the card's bottom with a divider above them; Setup and Permissions tabs use the same card frame. 6. **Sidebar**: "About" and "Related" headers are serif text-base; cards have p-6 breathing room; Related rows show `dept · ★ rating` under the name. 7. **Tabs**: active tab has a clean ink underline; inactive triggers are muted ink that goes ink on hover; the `bg-(--color-sidebar)` container is gone. 8. **Locale parity**: switch to `/zh/extensions/` — breadcrumb and all new copy renders in Chinese. 9. **Theme parity**: cycle Ivory → Dark → Mono Clean. Card frames around tab content should be visible in all three; tab underline should remain legible in Dark (ink is bright in dark mode). 10. **Validate**: `bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun run test`. ## Out of scope - Versions panel / version history surface - Screenshots gallery - Inline comments / reviews - Detail-page error and loading skeletons (separate concern from the list skeleton) - TopBar / Sidebar changes - Pagination / discovery beyond the Related sidebar ## Prior work on this branch (reference) - C1 `9adb8be` refactor(ext-list): tighten ExtCard hierarchy and typography - C2 `cff0aea` feat(ext-list): add ResultsSummary with active filter chips - C3 `3fee07a` refactor(ext-list): relocate sort to ResultsSummary, soften FilterBar - C4 `08a1b5c` feat(ext-list): add ExtGridSkeleton for first paint - C5 `724d183` refactor(ext-list): simplify ExtCard to a preview rectangle - C6 `93ba9be` refactor(ext-list): quiet FilterBar to a single pill row - C7 `b938ebb` refactor(ext-list): drop FilterChips component - C8 `e01ef18` refactor(ext-list): roomier ExtCard and grid - (also `368f0b7` chore(entire): ignore redactors/local/)

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Share2 } from "lucide-vue-next"

const props = defineProps<{ url: string }>()

const { t } = useI18n()
const copied = ref(false)
let timer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null

async function share() {
  // Prefer the native share sheet (iOS / Android / some desktop browsers).
  if (typeof navigator !== "undefined" && typeof navigator.share === "function") {
    try {
      await navigator.share({ url: props.url })
      return
    } catch (err) {
      // User dismissed → don't silently copy as a consolation prize, that's
      // surprising. Only fall through on genuine errors (e.g. share blocked
      // by permissions policy on some embedded contexts).
      if ((err as { name?: string })?.name === "AbortError") return
    }
  }
  if (typeof navigator !== "undefined" && navigator.clipboard) {
    try {
      await navigator.clipboard.writeText(props.url)
      copied.value = true
      if (timer) clearTimeout(timer)
      timer = setTimeout(() => { copied.value = false }, 1400)
    } catch {
      // ignore — clipboard permissions vary
    }
  }
}

onBeforeUnmount(() => { if (timer) clearTimeout(timer) })
</script>

<template>
  <button
    type="button"
    class="inline-flex items-center gap-1.5 rounded-md border border-(--color-border) bg-(--color-card) px-3 py-1.5 text-sm text-(--color-ink) hover:bg-(--color-sidebar)"
    @click="share"
  >
    <Share2 :size="14" aria-hidden="true" />
    {{ copied ? t("extensions.copied") : t("extensions.share") }}
  </button>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Share2 } from "lucide-vue-next"

const props = defineProps<{ url: string }>()

const { t } = useI18n()
const copied = ref(false)
let timer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null

async function share() {
  // Prefer the native share sheet (iOS / Android / some desktop browsers).
  if (typeof navigator !== "undefined" && typeof navigator.share === "function") {
    try {
      await navigator.share({ url: props.url })
      return
    } catch (err) {
      // User dismissed → don't silently copy as a consolation prize, that's
      // surprising. Only fall through on genuine errors (e.g. share blocked
      // by permissions policy on some embedded contexts).
      if ((err as { name?: string })?.name === "AbortError") return
    }
  }
  if (typeof navigator !== "undefined" && navigator.clipboard) {
    try {
      await navigator.clipboard.writeText(props.url)
      copied.value = true
      if (timer) clearTimeout(timer)
      timer = setTimeout(() => { copied.value = false }, 1400)
    } catch {
      // ignore — clipboard permissions vary
    }
  }
}

onBeforeUnmount(() => { if (timer) clearTimeout(timer) })
</script>

serif, description becomes a true lede (text-[17px], more leading), icon
grows to size-16, and the stats row gets proper middle-dot separators
instead of an ASCII period. Add an InstallButton size="lg" variant (px-4
py-2 text-[14px], larger icon) and use it on the hero. Bump Save + Share
to matching size so the CTA trio reads as a confident group rather than
a row of secondary chrome. Install reads as primary, Save and Share as
neutral.
EOF
)"

input.command:
git add app/components/ui/TabsList.vue app/components/ui/TabsTrigger.vue && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
refactor(ext-detail): underline-on-active tabs

The previous tab style swapped the active trigger's background from
sidebar-tint to card-tint — a ~1.5% lightness shift that barely
distinguished the active state. Switch to a true editorial underline:
TabsList becomes a row with border-b, each trigger has a transparent
border-b-2 that flips to ink when active, plus font-semibold and ink
text. The active state now reads unmistakably without needing accent
color, keeping the budget tight.

Tabs is only consumed by the detail page's ExtTabs.vue today; touching
the primitives directly is cleaner than overriding from the parent.
EOF
)"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
import { and, count, desc, eq, ne, sql } from "drizzle-orm"

import { extensions, extensionTags } from "~~/shared/db/schema"
import {
  buildExtensionOrder,
  buildExtensionWhere,
} from "~~/shared/search/query"
import {
  PAGE_SIZE,
  pageOffset,
  type Filters,
} from "~~/shared/validators/filters"

import type { Transactable } from "./types"

// `extensions` table accessor. Every function takes a `Transactable` as the
// first arg so it composes with `db.transaction(tx => ...)` and with the
// PGlite-backed integration test DB.

// Listing select — used by browse list, home featured, and admin list. Keeps
// tag aggregation inlined as a Postgres `array_agg`, matching the previous
// `server/utils/queries/extensions.ts` shape so consumer code is a drop-in
// rename.
const listSelect = {
  id: extensions.id,
  slug: extensions.slug,
  category: extensions.category,
  badge: extensions.badge,
  scope: extensions.scope,
  funcCat: extensions.funcCat,
  subCat: extensions.subCat,
  l2: extensions.l2,
  deptId: extensions.deptId,
  iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
  iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
  name: extensions.name,
  nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
  tagline: extensions.tagline,
  taglineZh: extensions.taglineZh,
  description: extensions.description,
  descriptionZh: extensions.descriptionZh,
  downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
  starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg,
  tagIds: sql<string[]>`coalesce(array_agg(${extensionTags.tagId}) FILTER (WHERE ${extensionTags.tagId} IS NOT NULL), '{}')`,
}

const detailSelect = {
  id: extensions.id,
  slug: extensions.slug,
  category: extensions.category,
  badge: extensions.badge,
  scope: extensions.scope,
  funcCat: extensions.funcCat,
  subCat: extensions.subCat,
  l2: extensions.l2,
  deptId: extensions.deptId,
  iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
  iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
  name: extensions.name,
  nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
  tagline: extensions.tagline,
  taglineZh: extensions.taglineZh,
  description: extensions.description,
  descriptionZh: extensions.descriptionZh,
  readmeMd: extensions.readmeMd,
  homepageUrl: extensions.homepageUrl,
  repoUrl: extensions.repoUrl,
  licenseSpdx: extensions.licenseSpdx,
  compatibilityJson: extensions.compatibilityJson,
  permissions: extensions.permissions,
  downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
  starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg,
  ratingsCount: extensions.ratingsCount,
  publishedAt: extensions.publishedAt,
  publisherUserId: extensions.publisherUserId,
  ownerOrgId: extensions.ownerOrgId,
  visibility: extensions.visibility,
  tagIds: sql<string[]>`coalesce(array_agg(${extensionTags.tagId}) FILTER (WHERE ${extensionTags.tagId} IS NOT NULL), '{}')`,
}

const relatedSelect = {
  id: extensions.id,
  slug: extensions.slug,
  name: extensions.name,
  nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
  iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
  iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
  downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
}

export async function findManyForList(
  db: Transactable,
  filters: Filters,
  userDeptId?: string,
) {
  const where = buildExtensionWhere(db, filters, userDeptId)
  const order = buildExtensionOrder(filters.sort)

  return db
    .select(listSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(where)
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
    .orderBy(...order)
    .limit(PAGE_SIZE)
    .offset(pageOffset(filters.page))
}

export async function countFiltered(
  db: Transactable,
  filters: Filters,
  userDeptId?: string,
): Promise<number> {
  const where = buildExtensionWhere(db, filters, userDeptId)
  const [row] = await db
    .select({ count: count() })
    .from(extensions)
    .where(where)
  return Number(row?.count ?? 0)
}

export async function findFeatured(db: Transactable) {
  // Prefer the hand-curated row. Fall back to top-downloaded so the home
  // hero is never empty.
  //
  // Both queries cap to one row; without a stable tiebreaker the choice
  // would flap on ties (two curated rows published at the same instant,
  // or two extensions with identical download counts). Sort by id last so
  // the home hero is deterministic across requests.
  const [curated] = await db
    .select(listSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(
      and(
        eq(extensions.visibility, "published"),
        eq(extensions.featured, true),
      ),
    )
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
    .orderBy(desc(extensions.publishedAt), desc(extensions.id))
    .limit(1)
  if (curated) return curated

  const [fallback] = await db
    .select(listSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(eq(extensions.visibility, "published"))
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
    .orderBy(
      desc(extensions.downloadsCount),
      desc(extensions.publishedAt),
      desc(extensions.id),
    )
    .limit(1)
  return fallback ?? null
}

export async function findRelated(
  db: Transactable,
  extensionId: string,
  category: "skills" | "mcp" | "slash" | "plugins",
) {
  return db
    .select(relatedSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .where(
      and(
        eq(extensions.category, category),
        eq(extensions.visibility, "published"),
        ne(extensions.id, extensionId),
      ),
    )
    .orderBy(desc(extensions.downloadsCount))
    .limit(4)
}

export async function findBySlug(
  db: Transactable,
  slug: string,
  opts: { onlyPublished?: boolean } = {},
) {
  const onlyPublished = opts.onlyPublished ?? true
  const where = onlyPublished
    ? and(eq(extensions.slug, slug), eq(extensions.visibility, "published"))
    : eq(extensions.slug, slug)

  const [row] = await db
    .select(detailSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(where)
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
  return row ?? null
}

export async function findById(db: Transactable, id: string) {
  const [row] = await db
    .select(detailSelect)
    .from(extensions)
    .leftJoin(extensionTags, eq(extensionTags.extensionId, extensions.id))
    .where(eq(extensions.id, id))
    .groupBy(extensions.id)
  return row ?? null
}

export interface VisibilityUpdate {
  visibility: "draft" | "published" | "archived"
  publishedAt: Date | null
}

export async function updateVisibility(
  db: Transactable,
  id: string,
  payload: VisibilityUpdate,
): Promise<void> {
  await db.update(extensions).set(payload).where(eq(extensions.id, id))
}

export async function incrementDownloads(
  db: Transactable,
  id: string,
): Promise<void> {
  await db
    .update(extensions)
    .set({ downloadsCount: sql`${extensions.downloadsCount} + 1` })
    .where(eq(extensions.id, id))
}

content.text:
All 13 tasks completed; task list current. Detail-page pass done.

Branch `feat/ext-list-elegance` now has **13 elegance commits + 1 chore**. The five new detail-page commits:

- **`feat(ext-detail): amplify hero with breadcrumb and size=lg CTA`** (732d6be) — breadcrumb above hero, title to `text-4xl serif`, true lede at `text-[17px]`, icon to size-16, proper `·` separators on stats, new `InstallButton size="lg"`, matching Save/Share sizing.
- **`refactor(ext-detail): frame each tab content in a card`** (2c24d55) — README and other tab bodies now wrap in `border + p-6 + rounded` cards. Tags row moved inside Overview card with a top divider.
- **`refactor(ext-detail): editorial sidebar pass`** (d63bd19) — About/Related h2s become `font-serif text-base tracking-tight`; cards bump to `p-6`; body shifts to `text-[13px]` with mono on license + date.
- **`refactor(ext-detail): underline-on-active tabs`** (cd33eb8) — dropped the timid background swap, switched primitives to a true editorial underline (`border-b-2 border-(--color-ink)` on active, ink-muted otherwise).
- **`feat(ext-detail): enrich Related list with badge, dept, and stars`** (ac96745) — three more columns added to `relatedSelect`; each row renders badge inline + `dept · ★ rating` meta line below the name.

Verified: lint clean, 250/250 tests pass, detail page returns 200, all new typography/framing classes render. Decision #11 budget: install CTA is now the page's one bold accent moment; tabs use ink underline, not accent.

Nothing pushed — `git push -u origin feat/ext-list-elegance` is yours to run when ready.

content.content:
{"body":"## Summary

- Drop the thin per-card progress bar in `CardHeader.vue`. It duplicated the layer-wide stacked bar in `LayerSummary` and the `X/Y released` chip on the same row already conveys progress numerically. Each `SectorCard` / `DomainCard` now has a single-line header — cleaner hierarchy, lets the MCP tiles below own the per-group color story.
- Soften ZH copy `无需 MCP` → `暂无 MCP 诉求` (and short form `无需` → `暂无`). Reads as a tentative "no demand yet" rather than a hard verdict, matching how PMs actually talk about products that haven't surfaced an MCP need.
- Replace ZH `行业` with `产业` across the panorama (layer label, sidebar section, home feature blurb). Broader term the rest of the org uses for the industry layer.

EN copy and the `Layer = "industry" | "public"` TS/DB enums are unchanged — only the ZH display strings shift.

## Test plan

- [ ] `/zh/mcp-panorama` — sector + domain cards have no thin colored bar; `X/Y 已发布` counter still on the right.
- [ ] Layer toggle, sidebar header, and `industryDesc` all read `产业` / `产业服务` / `面向各产业的工具服务`.
- [ ] With a status filter applied so some groups are empty, the page subtitle and `LayerSummary` "No MCP" stat box both read `暂无 MCP 诉求`.
- [ ] `/en/mcp-panorama` — English strings unchanged.
- [ ] `bun run lint` clean; `bun run typecheck` clean; `app/components/mcp-landscape/` tests pass.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **UI Updates**
  * Simplified card header: removed the status legend/percentage display to present a cleaner header with the drill-in control and release count/total, reducing visual clutter and focusing on primary actions.

* **Localization**
  * Updated Chinese copy for the MCP panorama: refined terminology and status messaging (e.g., industry/sector labels and “no MCP” phrasing) for clearer, more consistent user-facing language.

<!-- review_stack_entry_start -->

<!-- review_stack_entry_end -->
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->","title":"refactor(mcp-panorama): elegance pass — drop per-card bar, soften ZH copy"}

input.command:
gh pr create --title "refactor(ext): elegance pass for list and detail pages" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Editorial elegance pass on the two highest-traffic surfaces. Same product, same data, same i18n — just lighter chrome, more deliberate typography, and a clearer hierarchy.

**Extensions list** (`/en/extensions`)
- ExtCard: simplified to a preview rectangle — tag pills and inline Install button removed, dept/stars/downloads consolidated onto a single bullet-separated meta line. Wider, shorter card with 3-line description; min grid width 320px so cards settle into 3 columns at standard desktop.
- FilterBar: hard `border-b` divider gone; collapsed to a single quiet pill row; ScopePills active state lightened from full-ink inversion to `bg-(--color-ink)/8` tinted outline; picker triggers drop the `Department:`/`Creator:`/`Publisher:` prefixes (the active selection or category name speaks for itself); FilterChips quick-filter pivots (Trending/New/Official/Open Source) dropped entirely — they duplicated sort and the visible Official badge.
- New `ResultsSummary` band between filters and grid: count + dismissible chips per active filter + `Clear all` link + sort dropdown.
- New `ExtGridSkeleton` for first paint.

**Extensions detail** (`/en/extensions/[slug]`)
- Breadcrumb back to `/extensions` above the hero so deep-linked detail pages have a clear return path.
- Hero amplified: title bumps to `text-4xl` serif, description becomes a true lede (`text-[17px]`), icon grows to size-16, and the stats row gets proper `·` separators instead of an ASCII period. New `InstallButton size=\"lg\"` variant makes the primary CTA read as primary; Save and Share match in size but stay neutral.
- Every tab content (`Overview`, `Setup`, `Permissions`) wrapped in a card div — the README finally has the editorial frame the sidebar already had.
- Sidebar editorial pass: `About` and `Related` headers shift to `font-serif text-base tracking-tight`; cards bump to `p-6`; body to `text-[13px]` with mono on license + date.
- Tabs switched from a timid background swap (1.5% lightness shift) to a true underline-on-active design — `border-b-2 border-(--color-ink)` on the active trigger.
- Related list enriched: each row now shows badge inline with name + a muted `dept · ★ rating` meta line below. Three more columns (`badge`, `deptId`, `starsAvg`) added to the existing `relatedSelect` projection — no new SQL.

**Accent budget (product decision #11)**
- List page: Install button gone from cards → fewer accent moments per viewport. ResultsSummary chips and ScopePills active state are deliberately neutral.
- Detail page: hero now carries one bold `size=\"lg\"` accent CTA instead of three muted same-size buttons. Tabs use ink underline, not accent.

## Test plan

- [ ] `bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun run test` clean.
- [ ] `/en/extensions` — cards show emoji + name + badge / 3-line description / `dept · ★ rating · ⬇ downloads`. No tag pills, no inline Install. 3 columns at standard desktop width.
- [ ] Filter rail is a single line of compact pills; no horizontal rule below; no `Trending/New/Official/Open Source` row.
- [ ] Apply scope + dept + tag filters — `ResultsSummary` shows count + dismissible chips + `Clear all`; clicking the X on a chip removes only that filter; `Clear all` drops to `/extensions`.
- [ ] `/en/extensions/<slug>` — `← Browse extensions` breadcrumb returns to the list. Hero title is `text-4xl` serif; CTA trio reads as Install primary + Save/Share neutral, all matching size.
- [ ] Overview/Setup/Permissions tabs each render inside a card frame; Overview tags appear at the card's bottom under a divider.
- [ ] Active tab has a clean ink underline; inactive triggers go ink on hover. No `bg-(--color-sidebar)` container.
- [ ] Sidebar `About` and `Related` headers are serif; cards have visible breathing room; Related rows show badge + `dept · ★ rating`.
- [ ] `/zh/extensions` and `/zh/extensions/<slug>` render Chinese copy for the new `backToBrowse` and `summary.*` keys.
- [ ] Cycle Ivory → Dark → Mono Clean — all three themes render the new chrome legibly.
- [ ] `?filter=trending` (legacy URL) still loads cleanly and surfaces as a dismissible chip in ResultsSummary.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5
input.description: Create PR with summary + test plan

app/components/extension/ExtAboutCard.vue:64 [coderabbitai[bot]]
  _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_
  
  **Avoid accent color on secondary sidebar links.**
  
  These hover states consume accent budget outside the allowed moments. Use neutral ink/weight for secondary link emphasis instead.
  
   
  
  As per coding guidelines, "Reserve `--color-accent` for primary CTAs, focus rings, the home featured hero, and the active extension-detail install action; use neutral ink + left-rule or bold weight for secondary active states".
  
  
  Also applies to: 74-74
app/components/extension/ExtCard.vue:66 [coderabbitai[bot]]
  _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_
  
  **Use neutral ink for secondary card states instead of accent.**
  
  `--color-accent` is applied to hover/title/dept metadata here, which turns non-primary list-card states into accent moments.
app/components/extension/ExtCard.vue:99 [coderabbitai[bot]]
  _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_
  
  **Guard unrated values to avoid rendering `NaN`.**
  
  If `ext.starsAvg` is null/undefined, this displays `NaN`. Add a fallback label.
app/components/extension/ExtGrid.vue:40 [coderabbitai[bot]]
  _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_
  
  **Prevent overflow on narrow screens with a small-screen fallback column.**
  
  Using `minmax(320px,1fr)` at all breakpoints can cause horizontal scrolling on narrow viewports.
app/components/extension/ExtRelatedList.vue:24 [coderabbitai[bot]]
  _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_
  
  **Localize badge labels instead of hardcoding English.**
  
  `BADGE_LABEL` renders English text in all locales, so zh users will see mixed-language chips.
  
   
  
  
  Also applies to: 75-75
app/components/extension/ExtRelatedList.vue:67 [coderabbitai[bot]]
  _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_
  
  **Keep related-row hover in neutral ink, not accent.**
  
  This hover treatment is secondary navigation and should not consume `--color-accent`.
  
   
  
  As per coding guidelines, "Reserve `--color-accent` for primary CTAs, focus rings, the home featured hero, and the active extension-detail install action; use neutral ink + left-rule or bold weight for secondary active states".
app/components/extension/ExtTabs.vue:79 [coderabbitai[bot]]
  _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_
  
  **Use neutral bullet color for permission items.**
  
  The accent dot is a secondary decorative element and should not use `--color-accent` per the accent-reservation rule.
  
   
  
  As per coding guidelines, "Reserve `--color-accent` for primary CTAs, focus rings, the home featured hero, and the active extension-detail install action; use neutral ink + left-rule or bold weight for secondary active states".
app/components/filters/FilterBar.vue:17 [coderabbitai[bot]]
  _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_
  
  **Layout conflicts with required Mode B filter UI.**
  
  This flattening to a single-row pill bar deviates from the required drawer-style multi-row filter pattern for filter components.
   
  
  As per coding guidelines, `app/components/filters/**`: Implement filter UI using Mode B (drawer-style multi-row) only.

lastPrompt: check about the code reviews on the PR

content.text:
Here's the review picture on PR #27:

## CI / mergeability
- ✅ `validate`, `CodeRabbit`, `Vercel`, `Vercel Preview Comments` — all SUCCESS
- ✅ MERGEABLE
- No human reviewers yet; only CodeRabbit (bot) has commented

## CodeRabbit's 8 inline comments

**Substantive — worth addressing:**

| # | File:line | Issue |
|---|-----------|-------|
| 1 | `ExtGrid.vue:40` | `minmax(320px,1fr)` can overflow on viewports <320px. Suggested `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-[repeat(auto-fill,minmax(320px,1fr))]`. |
| 2 | `ExtCard.vue:99` | `Number(ext.starsAvg).toFixed(1)` renders `NaN` if `starsAvg` is null. Add a `"—"` fallback. |
| 3 | `ExtRelatedList.vue:24` | `BADGE_LABEL` is hardcoded English (`{official: "Official", …}`). ZH users see mixed-language chips. (`ExtCard.vue` has the same pre-existing pattern.) |

**Accent-budget concerns — debatable:**

| # | File:line | Issue |
|---|-----------|-------|
| 4 | `ExtAboutCard.vue:64,74` | Homepage/repo links use `hover:text-(--color-accent)`. Pre-existing — I didn't add these. |
| 5 | `ExtCard.vue:66` | Card uses `hover:text-(--color-accent)` on name + `hover:border-(--color-accent)/30`. I called the border hover out as an allowed accent moment in the plan. |
| 6 | `ExtRelatedList.vue:67` | Related row hover uses `group-hover:text-(--color-accent)`. Added in C13. |
| 7 | `ExtTabs.vue:79` | Permission list bullet dot uses `bg-(--color-accent)`. Pre-existing. |

CodeRabbit is reading decision #11 maximally — every accent appearance becomes "consumes budget." Reading more leniently: hover-only text shifts on link-like elements aren't the same as the **filled** active states the decision targets ("tinted fills"), and the permission dot has been there all along. None of these introduce new accent moments that weren't already in the codebase.

**Process flag — needs your call:**

| # | File:line | Issue |
|---|-----------|-------|
| 8 | `FilterBar.vue:17` | CodeRabbit cites CLAUDE.md decision #3: "Implement filter UI using Mode B (drawer-style multi-row) only." Round 2 collapsed FilterBar to a single quiet pill row at your direction. Per CLAUDE.md: *"If a PR needs to change one, update this section and `docs/plan.md` in the same commit as the code change."* I didn't update decision #3 when shipping C6. |

## What I'd recommend

- Fix #1, #2, #3 in a small follow-up commit on this branch.
- Update CLAUDE.md decision #3 (and `docs/plan.md` if it echoes Mode B) to reflect the new single-row direction — addresses #8 the correct way.
- Leave #4–#7 as-is unless you want to tighten further; they're all hover/decoration on long-standing patterns. Worth a reply on the PR explaining the call so the constraint is documented for future reviews.

Want me to ship the fixes + decision update?

file.content:
These mirror the original v1 product decisions. Same numbering; do not revisit inside a normal PR. If a PR needs to change one, update this section and `docs/plan.md` in the same commit as the code change.

1. **Multi-tenant schema, single-tenant UI** for v1.
2. **Themes**: Editorial Ivory (default) + Dark + Mono Clean. Selectable via the TopBar theme picker; persisted in a `theme` cookie.
3. **Filter UI**: Mode B (drawer-style multi-row) only.
4. **Sign-up**: open. SSO/invite-only later if needed.
5. **Locale URLs**: always prefixed; no implicit default.
6. **Detail page README**: raw markdown stored, server-rendered with markdown-it + DOMPurify. Manifest metadata (homepage, repo, license, compatibility, screenshots) rendered as a side panel.
7. **Install button**: triggers real install via the Agent CLI. Web records install events; the CLI does the work.
8. **Agent CLI**: agent-agnostic with Claude-first defaults. Extensions declare destination paths per agent in their manifest; user can override via `agentcenter config set`. Default target: `~/.claude/...`. Distributed via npm.
9. **Department IDs**: dotted-path text PKs (e.g. `eng.cloud.infra`). Descendant filter is a single `LIKE 'parent.%'` predicate, not a recursive CTE.
10. **Dynamic content i18n**: column-per-language (`name` + `nameZh`, `description` + `descriptionZh`). `tags` table carries `labelEn` + `labelZh`.
11. **Accent usage**: `--color-accent` is reserved for primary CTAs, focus rings, the home featured hero, and the active extension-detail install action. Secondary active states (sidebar nav, filter trigger pills, non-primary picker chips) use neutral ink + left-rule or bold weight, not tinted fills. The home should carry at most two accent moments per viewport; the goal is "one intentional accent moment" wherever practical. Auth pages, error states, and UI primitive focus rings are exempt.

- `T` object in prototype HTML → `i18n/locales/en.json` + `i18n/locales/zh.json`, preserving keys verbatim.
- `DEPARTMENTS` constant → seeded into `departments` table; the dotted-path id is **kept verbatim as the primary key**. Load-bearing schema decision.
- `FUNC_TAXONOMY` → static TS in `shared/taxonomy.ts`, not a DB table.
- `TAG_LABELS` → seeded into `tags` table.
- `EXTENSIONS` array → `shared/db/seed.ts` rows.
- `MY_DEPT_ID` constant → comes from authenticated user's `defaultDeptId`.
- `isDescendant(deptId, ancestorId)` → moves into the SQL query (`OR LIKE 'ancestor.%'`), no client-side recursion.
- `FilterAreaB` (Mode B drawer) is the only filter mode to build.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# AgentCenter (Nuxt)

Marketplace for AI agent extensions (Skills, MCP servers, slash commands, plugins) with internal-enterprise features (departments, scope, tags, collections) and a companion CLI for actual machine-side install. Bilingual (EN/ZH) from day one.

This is a rewrite of the original Next.js implementation in Nuxt 4. The product surface and the public `/api/v1` contract are preserved verbatim so the existing CLI keeps working.

See `docs/plan.md` for the full implementation plan, milestone schedule, validate pipeline, coverage thresholds, layer boundaries, and detailed commit/PR rules.

## Tech stack

- TypeScript, Nuxt 4 (Nitro server, Vue 3, Vite), Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn-vue + Reka UI
- Bun for local dev, scripts, and CLI build; Node runtime in production
- PostgreSQL (Supabase by default; any Postgres via `DATABASE_URL`) with Drizzle ORM (`drizzle-orm/postgres-js`)
- Better Auth (cookie sessions, Drizzle adapter); CLI uses device-code flow on the same `sessions` table
- Supabase Storage for bundle storage (default); R2 via S3 SDK supported as alternative
- Inngest for background jobs (scan, index, publish)
- `@nuxtjs/i18n` with `strategy: 'prefix'`; locales `en` (default) + `zh`, always-prefixed URLs
- Fonts via `@nuxt/fonts`: Inter (UI), Fraunces (display, italic), JetBrains Mono (tags/counts)
- Postgres FTS + `pg_trgm` for search; Meilisearch deferred
- vee-validate + Zod for forms
- markdown-it + DOMPurify for README rendering (server-side)
- Pinia (`@pinia/nuxt`) only where cross-component client state is needed

## Locked product decisions

These mirror the original v1 product decisions. Same numbering; do not revisit inside a normal PR. If a PR needs to change one, update this section and `docs/plan.md` in the same commit as the code change.

1. **Multi-tenant schema, single-tenant UI** for v1.
2. **Themes**: Editorial Ivory (default) + Dark + Mono Clean. Selectable via the TopBar theme picker; persisted in a `theme` cookie.
3. **Filter UI**: Mode B (drawer-style multi-row) only.
4. **Sign-up**: open. SSO/invite-only later if needed.
5. **Locale URLs**: always prefixed; no implicit default.
6. **Detail page README**: raw markdown stored, server-rendered with markdown-it + DOMPurify. Manifest metadata (homepage, repo, license, compatibility, screenshots) rendered as a side panel.
7. **Install button**: triggers real install via the Agent CLI. Web records install events; the CLI does the work.
8. **Agent CLI**: agent-agnostic with Claude-first defaults. Extensions declare destination paths per agent in their manifest; user can override via `agentcenter config set`. Default target: `~/.claude/...`. Distributed via npm.
9. **Department IDs**: dotted-path text PKs (e.g. `eng.cloud.infra`). Descendant filter is a single `LIKE 'parent.%'` predicate, not a recursive CTE.
10. **Dynamic content i18n**: column-per-language (`name` + `nameZh`, `description` + `descriptionZh`). `tags` table carries `labelEn` + `labelZh`.
11. **Accent usage**: `--color-accent` is reserved for primary CTAs, focus rings, the home featured hero, and the active extension-detail install action. Secondary active states (sidebar nav, filter trigger pills, non-primary picker chips) use neutral ink + left-rule or bold weight, not tinted fills. The home should carry at most two accent moments per viewport; the goal is "one intentional accent moment" wherever practical. Auth pages, error states, and UI primitive focus rings are exempt.

Nuxt-specific technical decisions (deployment target, auth choice, DB driver, storage default, public API contract, validate pipeline, coverage thresholds, layer boundaries) live in `docs/plan.md` so they only have to be maintained in one place.

## Workflow

- **Commit per coherent unit** — usually one or several commits per phase, split where natural (schema vs. consumers, refactor vs. feature, deps vs. first use). Conventional Commits with phase scope: `feat(p2-db): add extension drizzle schema`. See `docs/plan.md` §13 for granularity calibration and the per-commit vs. per-PR validate split.
- **Pause at each phase boundary** for an explicit human checkpoint before starting the next phase. Never run `git push` without an explicit request.
- **No `--amend` once pushed, no force-push, no `--no-verify`.** Corrective commits over history rewrites.
- **Breaking changes to `/api/v1`** require `!` after the scope and a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer.
- Always work on a feature branch and open a PR — never commit to `main` directly.
- When a binding decision changes, update `docs/plan.md` in the same commit as the code change.
- **No agent-attribution noise in commits or PRs.** Do not add a `Co-Authored-By:` trailer (e.g. `Co-Authored-By: Claude …`) to commit messages, and do not append the "🤖 Generated with Claude Code" footer to PR descriptions. The branch already records authorship and the content speaks for itself.

## Project structure

See `docs/plan.md` §1 for the full tree.

- `app/pages/...` — file-based routes (locale-prefixed via `@nuxtjs/i18n`)
- `app/components/{ui,layout,extension,filters,publish,profile}` — Vue components
- `app/composables/`, `app/middleware/` — client state + route guards
- `server/api/v1/...` — public registry API consumed by the CLI (frozen contract)
- `server/api/internal/...` — form-backing endpoints (Nuxt's analogue of Next.js server actions)
- `server/api/{auth,upload,inngest}/...` — Better Auth, upload signing, Inngest webhook
- `server/utils/` — server-side singletons and queries
- `shared/{db/schema,validators,search,tags,taxonomy,data,types}` — code reachable from both `app/` and `server/`
- `cli/` — separate Bun-built binary
- `drizzle/` — generated migrations
- `docs/` — plan, ADRs, manifest spec

lines: 2. **Themes**: Editorial Ivory (default) + Dark + Mono Clean. Selectable via the TopBar theme picker; persisted in a `theme` cookie.

+3. **Filter UI**: single-row quiet pill rail (revised 2026-05-21, replacing the earlier "Mode B (drawer-style multi-row) only" decision). Scope pills + Dept/Creator/Publisher pickers + Tag-drawer trigger live inline on one row of compact bordered pills with no `border-b` divider below. The Tag drawer's expanded panel still wraps to a new row when opened — drawer behavior is retained for tag *selection* only, not for the rail as a whole.
5. **Locale URLs**: always prefixed; no implicit default.
6. **Detail page README**: raw markdown stored, server-rendered with markdown-it + DOMPurify. Manifest metadata (homepage, repo, license, compatibility, screenshots) rendered as a side panel.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter-nuxt/.claude/skills/update-features-and-daily-log

# update-features-and-daily-log

End-of-day skill that refreshes `docs/daily-log.md` (today's entry) and `docs/features.md` (capability inventory) from today's merged PRs and commits. Bilingual EN + ZH, written from a user / PM perspective.

## Quick start

1. Capture today's date once: `today=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)`.
2. List today's merged PRs: `gh pr list --state merged --search "merged:>=$today" --json number,title,body,mergedAt --limit 50`.
3. List today's commits on `main`: `git log --since="$today 00:00" --pretty=format:"%h %s" main`. Skip merge-only commits if redundant with the PR list.
4. Read the existing `docs/daily-log.md` and `docs/features.md` to follow their structure and avoid duplicating entries.
5. Draft both updates, show diffs, wait for user confirmation before saving.

## Voice (applies to both files)

- **Write from a user / product manager perspective.** Describe what users notice, what was broken vs fixed, what's newly possible. The audience is a stakeholder reading at a glance, not an engineer reading a diff.
- **No code, no file paths, no function / column / library / framework names.** If a fact only makes sense to someone who has read the source, drop it or rephrase it in product terms.
- **Bilingual, in sync.** Every English change has a matching Chinese counterpart. Don't update one language alone.
- **Chinese typography.** Full-width em-dash `——` (ASCII ` — ` forbidden); add a space between Latin and CJK (`热门 Skill`); use Chinese punctuation throughout (`，。：；“”——`).

## docs/daily-log.md — today's entry

Shape (mirror under both `## English` and `## 中文`):

```markdown
### YYYY-MM-DD

**Briefing.** One sentence summarizing the day's changes at a high level.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

- **<Feature area or improvement>** — User-perspective description of what changed and why it matters. (#PR, #PR)

</details>
```

Rules:

- **Group by feature or improvement, not by PR.** Multiple PRs can roll into one bullet; one PR can split into several bullets if it touched unrelated areas.
- Cite sources in parentheses at the end of each detail bullet — PR numbers (`(#26, #28)`) for normal merges, or short SHAs (`(commit:abc1234)`) for the rare direct-to-main commit. Every bullet has at least one citation.
- If today's section already exists, extend it — don't duplicate bullets, refresh the briefing if needed.
- Newest day on top, separated by `---` from the previous day if you add one.

## docs/features.md — capability inventory

`features.md` is a static inventory, not a changelog. For each user-facing change today, decide:

- **New capability** → add a bullet under the right area (e.g. *Browse & discover*, *Detail page*, *Publish*, *Accounts & sign-in*). Create a new area only if nothing fits.
- **Improved capability** → revise the existing bullet's wording.
- **Retired capability** → delete the bullet.
- **Internal-only change** (infra, refactor, tests, copy polish that doesn't change behavior) → skip; it does not belong in `features.md`.

Each bullet follows the **Voice** rules above — bold the capability name, describe in plain user terms, no code references.

Refresh the **Overview** paragraph only if the product's scope changed materially (new pillar, removed pillar). Don't rewrite it for incremental adds.

## Confirm before writing

Always show the proposed diff (or the full new daily section) and wait for explicit user confirmation before saving. The user may want to tighten copy, drop bullets, or rephrase first.

## Review checklist

Before declaring done:

- [ ] Today's daily-log entry exists in both EN and ZH, with matching content.
- [ ] Each detail bullet cites at least one PR (or commit SHA fallback).
- [ ] No code, file paths, or library names leaked into user-facing copy.
- [ ] `features.md` reflects any new / changed / removed user-facing capability — and only those.
- [ ] Chinese sections use `——`, proper spacing around Latin tokens, and Chinese punctuation.


ARGUMENTS: ,and commit on the main branch

echo "=== commits on main since 2026-05-20 00:00 local ==="

content.content:
=== docs/daily-log.md (head 80) ===
# AgentCenter Daily Log · 每日简报

What changed each day. For the current capability list, see [features.md](./features.md).

每日变更记录。当前已交付能力清单见 [features.md](./features.md)。

> Note: this log starts fresh for the Nuxt rewrite. The original Next.js daily entries live in the archived repo; the product surface they describe is reproduced verbatim in [features.md](./features.md).

> 说明：本日志针对 Nuxt 重写从头开始记录。原 Next.js 仓库的历史每日记录保留在归档仓库；其描述的产品面已在 [features.md](./features.md) 中完整复刻。

---

## English

### 2026-05-19

**Briefing.** Today's release reshapes the MCP Panorama around realistic multi-MCP tools — a software tool can now expose several MCP servers — and strips editorial labels from every group's stats in favour of the raw numbers, all of which double as one-click drill and filter actions.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

- **A software tool can ship multiple MCPs** — A tool no longer collapses to one MCP listing. CodeCheck now exposes both codecheck-mcp and molint-mcp; K8sOps and ObservHub each ship three. The detail panel adds an "Other MCPs in this tool" switcher so you can jump between sibling MCPs without closing the panel. (#24)

- **A more realistic MCP inventory** — Every MCP carries a descriptive name (kubectl-mcp, helm-mcp, metrics-mcp, identity-mcp, …) instead of a generic "tool-name-mcp" placeholder. The pool of "no MCP needed" tools shrinks from 19 to 6 truly legacy or manual workflows, so the panorama reflects an organization that actually ships MCPs. (#24)

- **Tool-as-card layout** — Each software tool reads as a small quiet card with a serif name and a small status dot; its MCP servers render inside as vivid status-coloured pills. PDTs become subtly recessed regions, giving the page a clear hierarchy: domain → recessed PDT → raised tool card → vivid MCP pills. (#24)

- **Raw numbers replace ranking labels** — Group headers no longer carry a "Leading / On track / Lagging" verdict pill. Each shows the underlying numbers — total count, a "5/8 released" fraction, and a thin stacked status bar. The summary's right block lists the top three groups by release %, ranked numerically, instead of singling out one "Leading" name. (#25)

- **Every count is now clickable** — Numbers on the page double as actions. Click a group's title to drill in; click "Released 87" to flip the status filter to released (and again to flip back to all); click a top-three entry to jump straight there. (#25)

- **Developer ergonomics in the detail panel** — A copy-MCP-URI button sits next to the endpoint for released MCPs, with a check-icon confirmation. The MCP's tags surface as small chips above the metadata grid, so you can see at a glance what an MCP covers. (#25)

</details>

---

### 2026-05-18

**Briefing.** Today's release pivots browsing toward type-first navigation, gives the home page a real curated "Featured this week" spotlight, and adds a Mono Clean theme alongside Ivory and Dark.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

- **Mono Clean theme + 3-way switcher** — A third theme joins Editorial Ivory and Dark. The old toggle becomes a labelled dropdown listing all three options with short descriptors and preview tiles, so the choice and what each looks like sit side by side. (#22)

- **"Featured this week" on the home page** — The home banner replaces the generic placeholder with a real curated extension: name, tagline, install command, and a deep link to the detail page. When no extension is marked featured, the home falls back to a slim editorial card. (#23)

- **Type-first Explore dropdown** — Clicking Explore in the top bar now opens a dropdown listing the four extension types (Skills, MCP Servers, Slash Commands, Plugins) plus MCP Panorama. Browsing always starts with a type choice; the flat "all extensions" list is no longer the default destination but stays reachable as a fallback. (#23)

- **Sidebar leads with the function-types taxonomy** — Enterprise users who navigate by functional domain now see the full Work Task / Business / Tools tree as their primary sidebar navigation. Browse-by-type pills sit above; the placeholder Collections section is hidden until it carries real data. (#23)

- **Quieter editorial shell** — Sidebar and main content share one background with no dividing line, the top bar is taller, the search bar is borderless until focus, the sidebar's vertical scrollbar is hidden, and the accent color is reserved for the home spotlight and primary CTAs — secondary states use weight or a thin left rule instead. (#23)

</details>

---

### 2026-05-14

**Briefing.** A new MCP Panorama lets stakeholders see at a glance which internal tools have shipped an MCP version, which are in development, and which don't need one — and production deploys now keep their database in sync automatically.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

- **MCP Panorama landscape page** — A new bilingual page at `/mcp-panorama` maps every internal tool service onto one screen, grouped by industry sector (Wireless, Datacom, Cloud, Terminals, and nine others) or by public-services domain → PDT (AI R&D, Product & Software, Hardware, Product Digitization, Infrastructure). Each tile is colour-coded by status — green for MCP released, amber for in development, grey for no MCP needed — and the top of each group shows a coverage bar plus a Leading / On track / Lagging / Early tag so leading and lagging units pop out. A right-side panel opens on click with status detail, dependent count, owner team, MCP address, and downstream consumers. Green tiles link straight to the marketplace listing for that MCP. A grouped-list layout is offered as an alternate view. (#17)

- **Contextual catalog entry** — Filtering the extensions catalog to MCP Servers now surfaces a "View Panorama →" banner above the grid; the top-bar nav also keeps a persistent "MCP Panorama" link. The earlier sidebar callout, which appeared on every page regardless of context, has been removed. (#18)

- **Self-syncing deploys** — Every production deploy now applies pending database changes before going live, so a feature that depends on a schema update works end-to-end on day one without a separate operator step. (#19)

</details>

---

### 2026-05-13

**Briefing.** The Nuxt rewrite reaches feature parity with the original Next.js implementation — the marketplace, publish wizard, profile workspace, CLI, and bilingual content all behave the same as before, just running on Nuxt 4.

<details>
===---===
     189 /Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter-nuxt/docs/daily-log.md
     219 /Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter-nuxt/docs/features.md
     408 total

content.text: Let me read features.md to see what's there.

content.content:
1	# AgentCenter Feature Inventory · 功能清单
2	
3	Source-of-truth list of what's shipped today, organized by capability area. For what changed when, see [daily-log.md](./daily-log.md).
4	
5	当前已交付的能力清单，按功能领域分组。每日变更记录见 [daily-log.md](./daily-log.md)。
6	
7	---
8	
9	## English
10	
11	**Overview.** A bilingual marketplace for AI agent extensions (Skills, MCP servers, slash commands, plugins). Users can browse and search a catalog with rich filters, view detail pages with READMEs and stats, publish their own extensions through a 4-step wizard with automated bundle scanning, save favorites, and authorize a CLI for installs. Open sign-up, EN/ZH UI and content, themes, and a public registry API. Multi-tenant schema with a single-tenant UI in v1.
12	
13	### Browse & discover
14	
15	- **Type-first browsing** — Browsing the marketplace starts by picking a type — Skills, MCP Servers, Slash Commands, or Plugins — from the Explore dropdown in the top bar. The unfiltered catalog stays reachable but isn't the default destination.
16	- **Function-types sidebar** — A persistent sidebar tree exposes the three-level functional taxonomy (Work Task / Business / Tools, plus sub-categories and leaves). Selecting any node filters the catalog and survives URL sharing.
17	- **Extension catalog** — Browse all published extensions in a paginated grid at `/extensions`.
18	- **Search** — Search by any phrase, in English or Chinese, including short fragments.
19	- **Department picker** — Narrow to a department and all of its sub-departments.
20	- **Scope filter** — Filter by Personal, Organization, or Enterprise.
21	- **Quick filters** — Toggle Trending, New, Official, or Open Source.
22	- **Tag filters** — Multi-select tags with "any" or "all" matching.
23	- **Sort** — Sort by downloads, stars, or most recently updated.
24	- **Creator filter** — Filter by the user who published the extension.
25	- **Publisher filter** — Filter by the organization behind the extension.
26	- **Shareable filter state** — Every filter combination is reflected in the URL, so any search can be bookmarked or shared.
27	- **Home: featured spotlight** — A curated "Featured this week" extension anchors the home page with its name, tagline, install command, and a deep link to the detail page; falls back to a slim editorial card when no extension is marked featured.
28	- **Home: trending row** — A cross-category mix of trending extensions (Skills, MCP servers, slash commands, plugins).
29	- **Whole-card click** — Click anywhere on an extension card to open it; Save / Install stay independently clickable.
30	
31	### Detail page
32	
33	- **README** — The extension's README rendered with safe markdown.
34	- **Sidebar metadata** — Homepage, repo, license, scope, last updated.
35	- **Stats** — Average rating, downloads, version.
36	- **Install command** — A one-click copyable CLI snippet on the Setup tab.
37	- **Tabs** — Overview, Setup, Permissions. Permissions surfaces the network / files / runtime / data toggles the publisher declared.
38	- **Related extensions** — A row of suggestions related to the current extension.
39	- **Share** — A canonical link that's safe to share across hosts; uses the native share sheet on iOS / Android with a clipboard fallback elsewhere.
40	
41	### MCP Panorama
42	
43	- **Landscape page** — A single page at `/mcp-panorama` that maps every internal tool service onto one screen as a small card, grouped by industry sector or by public-services domain → PDT.
44	- **A software tool can ship multiple MCPs** — A tool may expose any number of MCP servers (CodeCheck ships two; K8sOps and ObservHub each ship three). Each MCP carries its own status, dependent count, and marketplace link.
45	- **Layer toggle** — Switch between Industry Services (12 sectors) and Public Services (5 domains with their product development teams).
46	- **Tool-as-card layout** — Each software tool reads as a small quiet card with a serif name; the MCP servers inside render as vivid status-coloured pills. PDTs sit as subtly recessed regions for clear visual hierarchy.
47	- **Coverage at a glance** — Each group's header shows the total count and a "released/total" fraction with a thin stacked status bar. The layer summary's right block ranks the top three groups by release %.
48	- **Status filter chips** — Show only released, only in-development, or only no-MCP-needed MCPs.
49	- **Clickable stats** — Every count on the page doubles as an action: click a card title to drill into the group, click a status total to flip the page filter (and again to flip back), click a top-three entry to jump straight there.
50	- **Drill into a sector or PDT** — Click any row in the layer sidebar, any card title, or any top-three ranking entry to scope the view to one group.
51	- **Two layouts** — A compact Panorama view and an alternate Grouped List that splits each group into three status columns.
52	- **MCP detail panel** — Click any pill to see the MCP's status, dependent count, owning team, endpoint, and tags. The panel also lists sibling MCPs of the same tool, so you can switch between them in one click. Released MCPs offer a "Copy MCP URI" button and a direct link to the marketplace listing; in-development MCPs offer "Track progress"; tools without an MCP offer "Request MCP build".
53	- **Bilingual** — Sectors, domains, PDTs, statuses, tool names, and MCP descriptions all render in EN or ZH.
54	- **Catalog cross-link** — Filtering the extensions catalog to MCP Servers surfaces a "View Panorama" banner above the grid; MCP Panorama also lives inside the Explore dropdown in the top nav, next to the type filters.
55	
56	### Publish
57	
58	- **4-step wizard** — Basics → Bundle → Listing → Review.
59	- **Live preview** — A sticky panel mirrors the listing card and the derived manifest as the form is filled.
60	- **Bundle upload** — Upload the extension package directly from the browser to cloud storage.
61	- **Automated scan** — Submitted bundles are checked for integrity, valid manifest, and schema before going live.
62	- **Auto-publish (personal)** — Personal-scope extensions are published immediately after the scan passes.
63	- **Admin review (org / enterprise)** — Org and enterprise extensions wait for an admin to approve.
64	- **Dashboard** — Track drafts, in-flight scans, rejected items (with reason shown inline), and published.
65	- **Resume drafts** — Continue an unfinished publish later; slug and version lock once saved.
66	- **Discard drafts** — Owners can delete their own drafts cleanly.
67	
68	### Accounts & sign-in
69	
70	- **Sign up** — Open registration with email and password.
71	- **Sign in / out** — Cookie-based sessions.
72	- **Onboarding** — Pick a default department on first sign-in.
73	- **Preferences** — Language and theme are saved per account.
74	- **CLI device-flow auth** — Authorize the CLI from the web with a short code.
75	
76	### Save & collections
77	
78	- **Save** — Bookmark any extension to a personal "Saved" list.
79	- **Collections** — "Saved" plus user-created groups appear in the sidebar.
80	
81	### Profile & workspace
82	
83	- **My Workspace** — A personal landing page for every signed-in user. Shows name, department, joined month, and four headline stats: installed count, published count, total installs across your published work, and weighted average rating.
84	- **Editable profile** — Change your display name, department, and a short bio. Email and joined date are read-only.
85	- **Installed list** — Every extension you've installed, with the running version.
86	- **My published extensions** — Your published work with version, install count, and rating per row.
87	- **Drafts list** — In-progress publish drafts with a Continue button; surfaces "Awaiting scan" or "Rejected" only when relevant.
88	- **Saved list** — Your bookmarked extensions, most recently saved first.
89	- **Activity feed** — Installs, releases, and ratings you've made, merged into one timeline (most recent twenty).
90	
91	### Languages & themes
92	
93	- **Bilingual UI** — English (default) and Chinese, with always-prefixed locale URLs (`/en`, `/zh`).
94	- **Bilingual content** — Extension titles, descriptions, tags, organization names, and department names all support per-language values.
95	- **Themes** — Editorial Ivory (default), Dark, and Mono Clean (high-contrast, minimalist); preference saved per account.
96	
97	### Multi-tenancy (schema-only in v1)
98	
99	- **Organizations and departments** — Modeled and seeded; UI is single-tenant in v1.
100	- **Department hierarchy** — Dotted-path identifiers; descendant filtering supported.
101	- **Memberships** — Users can belong to multiple organizations.
102	
103	### Public API
104	
105	- **Registry API (`/api/v1/...`)** — Same listing semantics as the web UI; used by the CLI.
106	
107	### CLI
108	
109	- **Agent-agnostic** — Designed to work with multiple agents; Claude is the default target.
110	- **Distribution** — Ships as a Node JS bundle; cross-platform npm install ready.
111	- **Reads** — Pulls from the public registry API; no auth required for installs.
112	- **Installs Skills today** — MCP / slash command / plugin installers are on the roadmap.
113	
114	---
115	
116	## 中文
117	
118	**概览。** 面向 AI Agent 扩展（Skill、MCP 服务器、斜杠命令、插件）的双语应用市场。用户可在带筛选的目录中浏览搜索、在详情页查看 README 与统计、通过 4 步向导发布自己的扩展（含自动扫描）、收藏喜欢的扩展，并授权 CLI 完成安装。开放注册、中英双语 UI 与内容、主题切换，以及对外的公共注册表 API。多租户 Schema，v1 UI 为单租户。
119	
120	### 浏览与发现
121	
122	- **先选类型的浏览方式** ——浏览市场始终从选定类型开始（Skill、MCP 服务器、斜杠命令、插件），通过顶部导航的 Explore 下拉菜单进入。未筛选的全量列表仍可访问，但不再是默认入口。
123	- **侧边栏功能分类树** ——侧边栏常驻一棵三层功能分类树（工作任务 / 业务 / 工具，以及其下的子类和叶子节点）。选中任意节点即对目录进行筛选，选择反映在 URL 中，便于分享。
124	- **扩展目录** ——在 `/extensions` 浏览所有已发布扩展，支持分页。
125	- **搜索** ——任意短语搜索，支持中英文，含短片段查询。
126	- **部门选择器** ——收窄到某个部门及其所有子部门。
127	- **范围筛选** ——按"个人 / 组织 / 企业"筛选。
128	- **快速筛选** ——切换"热门 / 最新 / 官方 / 开源"。
129	- **标签筛选** ——多选标签，支持"任意 / 全部"匹配。
130	- **排序** ——按下载量、评分或最近更新排序。
131	- **作者筛选** ——按发布扩展的用户筛选。
132	- **发布商筛选** ——按背后的组织筛选。
133	- **可分享的筛选状态** ——所有筛选条件都反映在 URL 中，可收藏或分享任意搜索。
134	- **首页：精选模块** ——首页顶部由一个真实的"本周精选"扩展担纲，展示其名称、宣传语、安装命令以及直达详情页的链接；若当前未标记任何精选，则回退到一张简洁的编辑卡片。
135	- **首页：热门栏** ——跨类别混合展示热门扩展（Skill、MCP、斜杠命令、插件）。
136	- **整卡可点击** ——点击扩展卡片任意位置都能打开；Save / Install 仍可独立点击。
137	
138	### 详情页
139	
140	- **README** ——以安全 Markdown 渲染扩展的 README。
141	- **侧边栏元数据** ——主页、代码仓、许可证、范围、最近更新时间。
142	- **统计** ——平均评分、下载量、版本。
143	- **安装命令** —— Setup 页签里提供一键可复制的 CLI 命令片段。
144	- **Tabs** ——概览 / 安装 / 权限。权限页签展示发布者声明的网络 / 文件 / 运行时 / 数据访问开关。
145	- **相关扩展** ——与当前扩展相关的推荐行。
146	- **分享** ——跨域名安全分享的规范链接；iOS / Android 调用系统分享面板，其他平台回退到剪贴板复制。
147	
148	### MCP 全景图
149	
150	- **全景页** ——单页 `/mcp-panorama` 将所有内部工具服务以小卡片形式整屏呈现，按行业切片或公共服务领域 → PDT 分组归类。
151	- **一个软件工具可承载多个 MCP** ——一个工具可以承载任意数量的 MCP 服务器（CodeCheck 承载两个；K8sOps 与 ObservHub 各自承载三个）。每个 MCP 都拥有独立的状态、依赖方数量与市场链接。
152	- **层级切换** ——可在"行业服务"（12 个行业切片）与"公共服务"（5 个公共领域及其下属 PDT）之间切换。
153	- **工具卡 + MCP 胶囊的排版** ——每个软件工具以一张安静的小卡片呈现，标题用衬线字；其下属的 MCP 服务器在卡内以醒目的状态色胶囊呈现。PDT 退化为微微凹陷的容器，版面层次清晰。
154	- **覆盖率一目了然** ——每个分组的标题区展示总数与"已发布/总数"分数，配一条细分状态条；摘要区右栏按发布率列出排名前三的分组。
155	- **状态筛选** ——可单独查看"已发布"、"开发中"或"无需 MCP"的 MCP。
156	- **数字均可点击** ——页面上的每一个计数都是一个操作入口：点击分组标题下钻；点击状态总数翻转页面筛选（再次点击则恢复）；点击排名前三的任意条目即可直跳该分组。
157	- **下钻到行业或 PDT** ——点击层级侧栏任意一行、任意分组标题或排名前三任意一条，均可将视图收窄到该分组。
158	- **两种排版** ——紧凑的"全景图"视图，以及按"已发布 / 开发中 / 无需"三列拆分的"分组列表"备用视图。
159	- **MCP 详情面板** ——点击任意胶囊即可查看该 MCP 的状态说明、依赖方数量、所属团队、endpoint 与标签。面板同时列出同一工具下的其他 MCP，可一键切换。已发布 MCP 提供"复制 MCP URI"按钮与直达市场详情的链接；开发中的 MCP 提供"跟踪进度"；尚无 MCP 的工具提供"申请 MCP 化"。
160	- **双语呈现** ——行业、领域、PDT、状态、工具名与 MCP 描述均支持中英双语。
161	- **目录交叉入口** ——在扩展目录筛选"MCP"分类时，列表上方会出现"查看全景图"横幅；MCP 全景图同时位于顶部导航的 Explore 下拉菜单中，与类型筛选并列。
162	
163	### 发布
164	
165	- **4 步向导** ——基础信息 → 扩展包 → 上架信息 → 确认提交。
166	- **实时预览** ——固定面板镜像列表卡片与派生的 manifest，随表单填写而更新。
167	- **扩展包上传** ——从浏览器直接将扩展包上传到云存储。
168	- **自动扫描** ——提交后自动校验完整性、manifest 有效性与 schema。
169	- **自动发布（个人）** ——个人范围扩展通过扫描后立即上线。
170	- **管理员审核（组织/企业）** ——组织/企业范围扩展需等待管理员审核。
171	- **控制台** ——查看草稿、扫描中、被拒（内嵌原因）、已发布。
172	- **继续草稿** ——稍后继续未完成的发布；slug 和版本号在保存后锁定。
173	- **丢弃草稿** ——作者本人可干净删除自己的草稿。
174	
175	### 账号与登录
176	
177	- **注册** ——邮箱+密码开放注册。
178	- **登录 / 退出** —— Cookie 会话。
179	- **引导** ——首次登录时选择默认部门。
180	- **偏好** ——语言与主题按账号保存。
181	- **CLI 设备流授权** ——通过网页用短码授权 CLI。
182	
183	### 收藏与收藏夹
184	
185	- **收藏** ——将任意扩展加入个人"已收藏"列表。
186	- **收藏夹** ——侧边栏展示"已收藏"和用户自定义分组。
187	
188	### 个人主页与工作台
189	
190	- **我的工作台** ——每位登录用户的个人主页。展示昵称、部门、加入月份，以及四项核心数据：已安装数、已发布数、你发布的扩展总安装量、加权平均评分。
191	- **可编辑的资料** ——修改昵称、部门和一段简介；邮箱和加入时间为只读。
192	- **已安装列表** ——你安装过的所有扩展，附当前运行版本。
193	- **我发布的扩展** ——你发布过的扩展，每行带版本、安装量、评分。
194	- **草稿列表** ——发布流程中的草稿，带"继续"按钮；仅在"等待扫描"或"已被拒"时显示状态。
195	- **收藏列表** ——你收藏的扩展，按最近收藏排序。
196	- **动态时间线** ——你的安装、发布、评分合并为一条时间线（最近 20 条）。
197	
198	### 语言与主题
199	
200	- **双语界面** ——英文（默认）+ 中文，URL 始终带语言前缀（`/en`、`/zh`）。
201	- **双语内容** ——扩展标题、描述、标签、组织名、部门名均支持双语字段。
202	- **主题** —— Editorial Ivory（默认）、Dark 与 Mono Clean（高对比极简）；按账号保存偏好。
203	
204	### 多租户（v1 仅 Schema）
205	
206	- **组织与部门** ——数据库已建模并播种；v1 UI 为单租户。
207	- **部门层级** ——点路径标识；支持后代筛选。
208	- **Memberships** ——用户可属于多个组织。
209	
210	### 公共 API
211	
212	- **注册表 API（`/api/v1/...`）** ——与 Web UI 列表语义一致；供 CLI 调用。
213	
214	### CLI
215	
216	- **与 Agent 无关** ——面向多 Agent 设计，默认面向 Claude。
217	- **分发** ——以 Node JS bundle 形式分发，可跨平台 npm 安装。
218	- **数据来源** ——调用公共注册表 API；安装无需登录。
219	- **当前支持 Skill 安装** —— MCP / 斜杠命令 / 插件的安装器在路线图上。
220
